For these reasons, we may share your site usage data with our analytics partners. By clicking “Accept,” you agree to our website's cookie use as described in our Cookie Policy. Summary Artificial Intelligence 2025 Legislation

Updated July 10, 2025
A Summary is a quick read to increase your knowledge of a topic.
Related Topic: Technology

AI—the use of computer systems to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as learning and decision-making—has the potential to spur innovation and transform industry and government. As AI advances and widespread adoption of these tools increase, government, business and the public are exploring the risks and benefits of using the system for different applications.

State governments across the country are starting to use or examine how AI can be used to improve government services such as enhancing customer service; improving health care facility inspections; and improving roadway safety. Legislators, industry and other stakeholders have engaged in robust discussions regarding the concerns about potential misuse or unintended consequences of AI.

More and more states have introduced AI-related legislation over the last few years. In the 2025 legislative session, all 50 states, Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, and Washington, D.C., have introduced legislation on this topic this year. Thirty-eight states adopted or enacted around 100 measures this year. Examples of those actions include:

  • Arkansas enacted legislation that clarifies who the owner of AI generated content is, which includes the person who provides data or input to train a generative AI model or an employer, if the content is generated as a part of employment duties. The new law specifies that the generated content should not infringe on existing copyright or intellectual property rights.

  • Montana's new, "Right to Compute," law sets requirements for critical infrastructure that is controlled by an AI system, such as instructing the deployer to develop a risk management policy that considers guidance from a list of specified standards, such as the latest version of the AI risk management framework from the national institute of standards and technology. The new law also specifies that the government cannot take actions that restrict the ability to privately own or make use of computational resources for lawful purposes, unless deemed necessary to fulfill a compelling government interest.

  • New Jersey adopted a resolution urging generative AI companies to make voluntary commitments regarding employee whistleblower protections.

  • New York enacted a new law that requires state agencies to publish detailed information about their automated decision-making tools on their public websites through an inventory created and maintained by the Office of Information Technology. The new law also amends the civil service law to strengthen worker protections, such as requiring when an AI system is used by the state government that it cannot affect the existing rights of employees pursuant to an existing collective bargaining agreement and requiring that an AI system does not result in displacement or loss of a position.

  • North Dakota's new law prohibits individuals from using an AI-powered robot to stalk or harass other individuals, expanding current harassment and stalking laws.

  • Oregon enacted a new law that specifies that a non-human entity including an agent powered by AI cannot use specific licensed and certified medical professionals' titles, such as a registered nurse and certified medication aide.

This webpage covers key legislation related to AI issues generally. Legislation related solely to specific AI technologies, such as facial recognition, deepfakes or autonomous vehicles, is being tracked separately.

Artificial Intelligence 2025 Legislation

Jurisdiction and Summary

Bill Number

Bill Title

Bill Status

Bill Summary

Category

Alabama

H 169 

Public Education

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to make appropriations for the support, maintenance and development of public education in state, for debt service, and for capital outlay for the fiscal year. Includes appropriations for AI, cybersecurity and STEM enhancements.

Appropriations; Education Use

Alabama

H 208 

State Government

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to secretary of Information Technology, duties cybertechnology technology quality assurance for state agencies further provided.

Government Use

Alabama

H 365 

Labor and Employment

Enacted

Relates to state Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics Council created in the Department of Workforce, membership and duties provided.

Studies; Education Use; Effect on Labor/Employment

Alabama

H 515 

Artificial Intelligence

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to AI; regulate use in health coverage decisions.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Alabama

H 516 

Consumer Protection

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to consumer protection, use of a computer to interact with a consumer when consumer believes the interaction is with a human is an unlawful trade practice.

Notification; Private Sector Use; Criminal Use

Alabama

H 557 

Health Insurance

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to health benefit plans; processes further specified for making coverage determinations with enforcement and oversight given to the Department of Insurance.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Alabama

HR 244 

Commending Ignite

Failed - Adjourned

Appoints committee to its leadership on their acquisition of reliant technologies.

 

Alabama

S 294 

Health Insurance

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to health insurance; regulates and modernizes the prior authorization process.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Alaska

H 53 

Appropriations for Operating and Loan Program

Enacted

Makes appropriations for the operating and loan program expenses of state government and for certain programs; capitalizes funds; amends appropriations; makes supplemental appropriations. Includes appropriations for AI projects. Requires the Office of Information Technology to present a prioritized plan to the co-chairs of the finance committees and to the Legislative Finance Division by Dec. 20, 2025 for the uses, costs, and expected benefits of projects using AI.

Government Use; Appropriations

Alaska

HCR 3 

Artificial Intelligence Legislative Task Force

Pending - Carryover

Establishes the Joint Legislative Task Force on AI.

Studies; Government Use; Energy; Private Sector Use; Education/Training; Effect on Labor/Employment

Alaska

S 2 

Deepfake Disclosure

Pending - Carryover

Relates to the disclosure of election related deepfakes; relates to use of AI by state agencies; relates to the transfer of data about individuals between state agencies.

Elections; Government Use; Notification; Impact Assessment; Criminal Use

Alaska

S 33 

Synthetic Media Liability

Pending - Carryover

Relates to defamation claims based on the use of synthetic media; relates to the use of synthetic media in electioneering communications.

Elections; Criminal Use; Notification

Alaska

S 64 

Elections

Pending - Carryover

Relates to elections; voters; voting; voter preregistration for minors at least 16 years of age; voter registration; the Alaska Public Offices Commission; synthetic media in electioneering communications; campaign signs; public official financial disclosures; and the crime of unlawful interference with voting in the first degree. Provides for an effective date.

Elections; Notification; Criminal Use

Arizona

H 2175 

Claims and Prior Authorization

Enacted

Provides that before a health care insurer may deny a claim submitted by a provider on the basis of medical necessity, the medical director shall individually review the denial. Provides that during each individual review, the medical director shall exercise independent medical judgment and may not rely solely on recommendations from any other source.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Arizona

H 2342 

Blockchain Technology

Enacted

Relates to blockchain technology; provides that a city or town may not prohibit or otherwise restrict an individual from lawfully accessing or using computational power or running a node on blockchain technology in a residence; and provides that a county may not prohibit or otherwise restrict an individual from lawfully accessing or using computational power or running a node on blockchain technology in a residence.

Government Use; Oversight/Governance; Private Sector Use

Arizona

H 2678 

Indistinguishable and Visual Depiction and Definition

Enacted

Relates to the sexual exploitation of children; provides that a dangerous crime against children shall include a visual depiction that is indistinguishable from an actual minor that is under the specified age; provides that a visual depiction includes each visual image that is contained in, among other things, a photograph or data, regardless of whether the image is created or modified by software, AI or other digital editing tools, and that is capable of conversion into a visual image.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

Arizona

H 2712 

Indistinguishable and Visual Depiction

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to indistinguishable; relates to visual depiction; relates to minor; relates to definition.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

Arizona

H 2847 

Rental Price Fixing and Algorithmic Pricing

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to rental price fixing; relates to algorithmic pricing.

Housing; Private Sector Use

Arizona

S 1140 

Telehealth Program and Homeless and Recovery Services

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to telehealth program; relates to homeless; relates to recovery services.

Government Use; Health Use; Appropriations

Arkansas

H 1041 

Deceptive and Fraudulent Deepfakes in Elections 

 

Failed

Prohibits deceptive and fraudulent deepfakes in election communications; provides for a cause of action and civil penalties for the use of deceptive and fraudulent deepfakes and for other purposes; provides that a person may, within 90 days of an election in which a candidate for an elected office will appear on the ballot, distribute synthetic media that the person knows or should know is a deceptive and fraudulent deepfake of a candidate or party on the state or local ballot if the synthetic media.

Elections; Notification; Criminal Use

Arkansas

H 1071 

Frank Broyles Publicity Rights Protection Act

Enacted

Provides protections for an individual whose photograph, voice or likeness is reproduced through means of AI and used commercially; provides that it is not a violation of the Frank Broyles Publicity Rights Protection Act of 2016 if the name, voice, signature, photograph or likeness of an individual is used by a service provider of a system or network if the service provider does not have actual knowledge that a photograph, voice or likeness on the system or network is in violation.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Criminal Use; Private Sector Use

Arkansas

H 1083 

Arkansas Kids Online Safety Act

Failed - Adjourned

Provides that a covered platform shall take reasonable measures in the design and operation of any product, service, or feature that the platform knows is used by minors to avoid any heightened risk of harm to minors, including anxiety, depression, and suicidal behaviors; provides that parental tools provided by a covered platform shall include the ability to manage a minors privacy and account settings; creates the Kids Online Safety Council; provides for filter bubble transparency requirements.

Private Sector Use; Notification

Arkansas

H 1141 

Criminal Offenses

Failed

Creates criminal offenses related to deceptive and injurious media in the course of election campaigns; provides that it shall be unlawful for a person to purposely injure a candidate for office in the state by creating or causing to be created a deepfake and publishing, distributing, or causing to be published or distributed the deepfake a specified number of days before the date of the general election and on the date of the general election with the knowledge that the communication was a deep fake.

Elections; Criminal Use

Arkansas

H 1283 

Division of Elementary

Failed

Requires the division of elementary and secondary education to include digital media standards in kindergarten through grade 12 library media standards.

Education/Training

Arkansas

H 1297 

AI Algorithms

Failed - Adjourned

Concerns AI, algorithms, and other automated technologies; regulates certain practices of healthcare insurers.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Notification; Provenance

Arkansas

H 1518 

Offense of Creating Distributing Possessing or Viewing

Failed - Adjourned

Creates the offense of creating, distributing, possessing, or viewing AI-generated matter depicting sexually explicit conduct involving a child.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

Arkansas

H 1816 

Health Care Providers

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits health care providers and health care insurers from using AI in the delivery of health care services or the generation of medical records unless certain requirements are met.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Arkansas

H 1823 

Disrupt Explicit Forged Images and Nonconsensual Edits

Failed

Establishes the Disrupt Explicit Forged Images and Nonconsensual Edits Act of 2025; clarifies the rights for an individual affected by nonconsensual activities involving intimate digital forgeries

Criminal Use; Private Right of Action; Child Pornography

Arkansas

H 1876 

Model Training and Content Generated by Generative AI

Enacted

Relates to the ownership of model training and content generated by a generative AI tool; provides that, with certain exceptions, when a person uses a generative AI tool to generate content, the person who provides the input or directive to the generative AI tool shall be the owner of the generated content, provided that the content does not infringe on existing copyrights or intellectual property rights.

Provenance; Private Sector Use

Arkansas

H 1877 

Criminal Offenses

Enacted

Concerns criminal offenses related to possession of sexually explicit material that depicts a child; includes a computer-generated image that is indistinguishable from the image of a child.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

Arkansas

H 1958 

Public Entities to Create a Policy

Enacted

Requires public entities to create a policy concerning the authorized use of AI.

Government Use

Arkansas

S 612 

Private Right of Action Against a Social Media Platform

Enacted

Creates a private right of action for the use of a design, algorithm, or feature by a social media platform that causes harm; imposes a civil penalty on a platform that knowingly and willfully contributes to the suicide or attempted suicide of a minor; provides that a platform is liable for an award of litigation costs, attorneys fees, and damages in an action brought by a parent or guardian whose child or legal dependent commits suicide or attempts suicide following exposure to certain online content.

Private Sector Use; Private Right of Action

California

A 101 

Budget Act of 2025

Pending

Makes appropriations for the support of state government for the specified fiscal year. Requires generative AI projects in state departments, agencies, or other entities under the control of the governor to be approved in the Budget Act or another statute.

Appropriations; Government Use

California

A 102 

Budget Act of 2025

Enacted

Provides that the Budget Act of 2025 would make appropriations for the support of state government for the specified fiscal year. Amends the Budget Act of 2025 by amending, adding, and repealing items of appropriation and making other changes. Provides for the support of, among other things, the Legislative Analyst's Office, the State Court Facilities Construction Fund, the Judicial Branch, local assistance, and the Governor's Office of Business and Economic Development.

Government Use; Appropriations

California

A 103 

Budget Acts of 2022, 2023, and 2024

Pending

Provides that the Budget Act of 2022, the Budget Act of 2023, and the Budget Act of 2024 made appropriations for the support of State government for the specified fiscal years. Amends those budget acts by amending, adding, and repealing items of appropriation and making other changes.

Appropriations; Education Use; Education/Training

California

A 222 

Data Centers: Energy Usage Reporting and Efficiency

Pending

Requires a developer of a generative AI system, before using a covered model commercially or before making a covered model available for use by a third party, to calculate the total energy used to develop the covered model and the percentage of the total energy used to develop the covered model that was generated in the state. Requires the State Energy Resources Conservation and Development Commission to include energy consumption trends for data centers in its energy policy reports.

Energy; Private Sector Use

California

A 279 

School Libraries: Model Library Standards

Pending

Provides that existing law requires the State Board of Education to adopt standards, rules and regulations for school library services. Requires the superintendent of public instruction to consider recommending revisions to the standards for school library services to the board. Requires, if the superintendent decides to recommend revisions, the superintendent to convene a group of experts. Requires the superintendent, in consultation with the Instructional Quality Commission, to hold public hearings.

Education/Training

California

A 316 

AI: Defenses 

 

Pending

Prohibits a defendant that developed or used AI, as defined, from asserting a defense that the AI autonomously caused the harm to the plaintiff.

Private Sector Use; Private Right of Action

California

A 325 

Cartwright Act: Violations

Pending

Relates to the Cartwright Act. Makes it unlawful for a person to use or distribute any pricing algorithm that uses, incorporates, or was trained with data other than nonpublic competitor data if the person either distributes the pricing algorithm or uses the pricing algorithm to set or recommend a price or commercial term of a product or service, and the pricing algorithm was used by another person to set or recommend a price or commercial term of a product or service in the same market or a related market.

Private Sector Use

California

A 410 

Bots: Disclosure

Pending

Requires a person who uses a bot to autonomously communicate with another to ensure that the bot discloses to any person with whom the bot communicates when the bot first communicates with the person that the bot is a bot and not a human being. Authorizes the attorney general, a district attorney, county counsel, city attorney, or city prosecutor to bring a civil action to punish noncompliance.

Private Sector Use; Notification; Criminal Use

California

A 412 

Generative AI: Training Data

Pending

Requires a developer of a generative AI model to, among other things, document any copyrighted materials that the developer knows were used to train the model. Requires the developer to make available a mechanism on the developer's website allowing a copyright owner to submit a request for information about the developer's use of copyrighted materials that would allow the copyright owner to provide the developer with, among other things, registration numbers and fingerprints.

Provenance; Private Sector Use

California

A 489 

Health Care Professions: Deceptive Terms or Letters

Pending

Prohibits AI or generative AI technology from using certain terms, letters or phrases that indicate or imply that certain advice, care, reports or assessments being provided through AI or generative AI are being provided by a natural person with the appropriate health care license or certificate.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

California

A 502 

 

Pending

Sets forth additional requirements relating to the color and font size of the disclosure's text. Exempts an election communication from the prohibition if the content includes a disclosure stating that the communication has been generated or substantially altered using AI. Exempts an advertisement or other election communication from these prohibitions if a reasonable person would understand that the content was satire or parody.

Elections; Notification

California

A 512 

Health Care Coverage: Prior Authorization

Pending

Provides that existing law generally authorizes a health care service plan or disability insurer to use utilization review. Shortens the timeline for prior authorization requests to no more than a specified number of hours for standard requests or a specified number of hours for urgent requests from the plan's or insurer's receipt of the information reasonably necessary and requested by the plan or insurer to make a determination.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

California

A 682 

Health Care Coverage Reporting

Pending

Requires the financial reports submitted by health insurers to include specified information for each month, including the total number of claims processed, adjudicated, denied, or partially denied.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

California

A 723 

Real Estate: Digitally Altered Images: Disclosure

Pending

Requires a real estate broker or salesperson who includes a digitally altered image in an advertisement or other promotional material for the sale of real property to include a specified disclosure. Requires, if the advertisement or promotional material is posted on an internet website, the real estate broker or salesperson to additionally include the same image without digital alteration.

Private Sector Use; Provenance; Notification; Housing

California

A 853 

California AI Transparency Act

Pending

Relates to the State AI Transparency Act. Requires a large online platform to, among other things, retain any available provenance data in content provided to, or posted on, the platform. Requires a capture device manufacturer, with respect to any capture device the capture device manufacturer produces for sale in the state, to, among other things, include in the capture device's default capture app the ability for a user to enable the inclusion of provenance data in the user's captured content.

Provenance; Notification; Private Sector Use

California

A 887 

Pupil Instruction: High Schools: Computer Science

Pending

Requires school districts and charter schools to describe in the adopted plan their planned efforts to increase the computer science course enrollment of female pupils, pupils with disabilities, pupils who belong to ethnic and racial groups, and pupils eligible for free or reduced-priced meals that are underrepresented in the field of computer science.

Education/Training

California

A 979 

California Cybersecurity Integration Center: Artificial

Pending

Requires the State Cybersecurity Integration Center to develop, in consultation with the Office of Information Security and the Government Operations Agency, a State AI Cybersecurity Collaboration Playbook to facilitate information sharing across the AI community and to strengthen collective cyber defenses against emerging threats. Requires the center to review federal requirements, standards and industry best practices.

Studies; Cybersecurity

California

A 1018 

Automated Decision Systems

Pending

Relates to automated decision systems (ADS). Requires a deployer of a covered ADS to take certain actions, including providing certain disclosures to a subject of a consequential decision made or facilitated by the covered ADS, providing the subject an opportunity to opt out of the use of the covered ADS, providing the subject with an opportunity to appeal the outcome of the decision, and submitting the covered ADS to third party audits. Prescribes requirements for a third party to audit a covered ADS.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use; Notification; Audit; Impact Assessment; Responsible Use; Education/Training; Housing; Health Use

California

A 1024 

Dept. of Fish and Wildlife: San Gabriel Valley

Pending

Establishes the San Gabriel Valley Bear Management and Community Safety Act, which would require, on or before Jan. 1, 2027, the Department of Fish and Wildlife to develop a regional plan specific to the cities located within the San Gabriel Valley in the County of Los Angeles to address issues relating to bears in the community. Requires the plan to include various components, including, among other things, an overview of the behaviors of the bears in the San Gabriel Mountains.

Government Use

California

A 1064 

Leading Ethical AI Development (LEAD) for Kids Act

Pending

Provides for the Leading Ethical AI Development for Kids Act. Establishes the LEAD for Kids Standards Board in the Government Operations Agency. Defines covered product to mean an AI system that is intended to, or highly likely to, be used by children, used to process a child's personal information, or applied directly to a child. Requires the board to adopt regulations governing criteria for developers to determine if an AI system is a covered product.

Private Sector Use

California

A 1137 

Reporting Mechanism: Child Sexual Abuse Material

Pending

Provides that existing law requires a social media platform to take certain actions with respect to child sexual abuse material on the social media platform, including by requiring the platform to provide a means for a user who is a state resident to report material. Requires a social media company to ensure review by a natural person if there is not an established or known hash match to child sexual abuse material with respect to the reported material and the reported material is not otherwise blocked.

Child Pornography; Private Sector Use

California

A 1159 

Student Personal Information

Pending

Applies the provisions of the K-12 Pupil Online Personal Information Protection Act and the Early Learning Personal Information Protection Act to an operator of a website, online service, online application, or mobile application with actual knowledge that the site, service, or application is used for the applicable school purposes and was designed or marketed for those purposes. Enacts the Higher Education Student Information Protection Act. Authorizes a harmed student or pupil to bring a civil action.

Education Use; Private Sector Use

California

A 1242 

Language Access 

 

Pending

Requires each state agency to conduct an assessment and survey of the language needs of non-English speaking and limited English-speaking people. Requires a state agency to utilize specified information in conducting the assessment and survey and in developing and updating the implementation plan regarding the Dymally Alatorre Bilingual Services Act, including, among other things, the most recent census data from the United States Census Bureau.

Government Use

California

A 1331 

Workplace Surveillance

Pending

Limits the use of workplace surveillance tools by employers, including by prohibiting an employer from monitoring or surveilling workers in employee-only, employer-designated areas. Provides workers with the right to leave behind workplace surveillance tools that are on their person or in their possession during off-duty hours. Subjects an employer who violates the bill to a specified civil penalty and authorizes a public prosecutor to bring specified enforcement actions.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use

California

A 1337 

Information Practices Act of 1977

 

Pending

Provides that the Information Practices Act of 1977 prescribes a set of requirements, prohibitions, and remedies applicable to agencies with regard to their collection, storage, and disclosure of personal information. Provides that existing law exempts from such provisions counties, cities, school districts, municipal corporations, districts, political subdivisions, and other local public agencies. Removes that exemption for local agencies. Revises and expands the definition of personal information.

Government Use

California

A 1405 

Artificial Intelligence: Auditors: Enrollment

Pending

Requires the Government Operations Agency to establish a mechanism on the agency's website allowing AI auditors to enroll with the agency and allowing natural persons to report misconduct by an enrolled AI auditor. Requires the agency to publish information provided by an enrolled AI auditor on the agency's website, retain specified reports for a specified period, and share reports submitted by persons reporting misconduct with other state agencies for enforcement purposes.

Government Use; Audit

California

S 7 

Employment: Automated Decision Systems 

 

Pending

Requires an employer, or a vendor engaged by the employer, to provide a written notice that an automated decision system, for the purpose of making employment-related decisions, is in use at the workplace to all workers that will be directly or indirectly affected by the ADS. Requires the employer or vendor to maintain a list of all automated decision systems currently in use. Requires the notice to include the updated list.

Government Use; Effect on Labor/Employment; Notification

California

S 11 

Artificial Intelligence Technology

Pending

Provides that existing law creates a civil cause of action against any person who knowingly uses the name, voice, signature, photograph, or likeness of another person, without their consent, for specified purposes. Clarifies that, for purposes of this cause of action, a digital replication of a voice or likeness that a reasonable person would believe to be a genuine voice or likeness is deemed to be the voice or likeness of the person depicted.

Judicial Use

California

S 52 

Housing Rental Rates and Occupancy Levels: Algorithmic

Pending

Prohibits a person engaged in the business of providing a rental pricing algorithm that is used to set rental rates, lease terms, or occupancy rates for residential premises and that is not otherwise prohibited from using nonpublic competitor data pertaining to residential premises in the state.

Private Sector Use; Housing

California

S 53 

CalCompute: Foundation Models: Whistleblowers

Pending

Establishes within the Government Operations Agency a consortium required to develop a framework for the creation of a public cloud computing cluster to be known as CalCompute that advances the development and deployment of AI that is safe, ethical, equitable and sustainable. Requires the agency to submit a report from the consortium to the legislature with that framework. Provides for dissolving the consortium upon submission of that report. Provides protection for whistleblowers.

Private Sector Use; Government Use; Studies; Impact Assessment ; Cybersecurity

California

S 57 

Electrical Corporations: Tariffs

Pending

Provides for the Ratepayer and Technological Innovation Protection Act. Requires the Public Utilities Commission to establish or modify a special electrical corporation tariff for transmission and distribution service to eligible customers that, among other things, ensures just and reasonable rates for customers of electrical corporations and does not result in cost shifts to customers who do not receive the tariff.

Private Sector Use; Energy

California

S 69 

Artificial Intelligence Program: Attorney General

Pending

Requires the attorney general to establish and maintain a specified program to build internal expertise in AI, including its applications, risks, regulatory implications, and civil rights impacts. Requires, on or before the specified date, and annually thereafter, the attorney general to submit a public report to the Legislature describing the program, key developments in AI law and policy, and recommendations for additional State oversight or safeguards.

Judicial Use

California

S 101 

Budget Act of 2025

Enacted

Makes appropriations for the support of the government of the state of California and for several public purposes in accordance with the provisions of Section 12 of Article IV of the Constitution of the State of California, relating to the state budget, to take effect immediately, budget bill. Includes appropriations for state agency use of AI. Requires AI proposals to be approved in the Budget Act or another statute in order to implement AI projects in state departments.

Government Use; Appropriations

California

S 102 

Budget Act of 2025

Pending

Provides that the Budget Act of 2025 would make appropriations for the support of state government for the specified fiscal year. Amends the Budget Act of 2025 by amending, adding and repealing items of appropriation and making other changes.

Appropriations

California

S 103 

Budget Acts of 2022, 2023, and 2024

Enacted

Provides that the Budget Act of 2022, the Budget Act of 2023, and the Budget Act of 2024 made appropriations for the support of state government for the specified fiscal years. Amends those budget acts by amending, adding, and repealing items of appropriation and making other changes.

Appropriations; Education Use

California

S 238 

Workplace Surveillance Tools

Pending

Requires an employer to annually provide a notice to the Department of Industrial Relations, in the Labor and Workforce Development Agency, of all the workplace surveillance tools the employer is using in the workplace. Requires the notice to include, among other information, the data that will be collected from workers and consumers and whether they will have the option of opting out of the collection of personal data.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use

California

S 241 

Community Colleges: Personnel: Qualifications

Pending

Relates to the California Community Colleges. Requires a community college instructor, librarian, counselor, student personnel worker, supervisor, administrator, chief administrative officer, extended opportunity programs and services worker, disabled students programs and services worker, apprenticeship instructor, or supervisor of health to be a person who meets specified minimum qualifications to serve in that position or the alternative qualifications to serve in that position.

Education Use; Education/Training

California

S 243 

Companion Chatbots

Pending

Requires an operator of a companion chatbot platform to take reasonable steps to prevent a chatbot on its platform from providing rewards to a user at unpredictable intervals or after an inconsistent number of actions or from encouraging increased engagement, usage, or response rates. Requires an operator to annually report to the Office of Suicide Prevention the number of times the operator has detected exhibitions of suicidal ideation by users. Requires the office to post certain data on its website.

Private Sector Use; Notification; Private Right of Action

California

S 253 

State Bar of California

Pending

Provides that existing law requires certain revenue received from certain noninsurance and insurance affinity programs offered to licensees of the State Bar to go to the California Lawyers Association or an affiliated 501(c)(3) organization to support diversity, equity and inclusion. The bill would require the Committee of Bar Examiners to give notice if certain changes are made to the bar examination, including changing the functionality of testing software or materials, or if AI is used in creating questions for or grading the bar examination.

Education Use; Judicial Use

California

S 259 

Fair Online Pricing Act 

 

Pending

Provides for the Fair Online Pricing Act. Prohibits a price offered to a consumer through the consumer's online device from being generated based on certain input data, including the presence or absence of any software on the online device. Requires, if an online device cannot display a certain link because it lacks a physical display, a person to create an alternate mechanism for providing access to the list of input data.

Private Sector Use

California

S 295 

California Preventing Algorithmic Collusion Act of 2025

Pending

Enacts the California Preventing Algorithmic Collusion Act of 2025 to require a person, upon request of the attorney general, to provide to the attorney general information related to pricing algorithms. Requires specified officers to certify the accuracy of the report, subject to civil penalties. Prohibits a person from using or distributing any pricing algorithm that uses, incorporates, or was trained with, competitor data.

Private Sector Use

California

S 366 

Employment: Artificial Intelligence

Pending

Requires the Department of General Services to contract with the University of California, Los Angeles Labor Center to conduct a study evaluating the impact of AI on worker well-being, job quality, job types, different populations, and state revenues. Requires the department, on or before the specified date, to submit a report of the findings of the study to the Legislature. Repeals these provisions upon submission of that report.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Studies; Government Use

California

S 384 

Preventing Algorithmic Price Fixing Act: Prohibition

Pending

Provides for the Preventing Algorithmic Price Fixing Act, which would, in addition to any other law, prohibit a seller from using a price fixing algorithm to set either a price or supply level of a good or service or a rent or occupancy level of rental property. Authorizes the attorney general or a city attorney or county counsel to file a civil action for violations of these provisions, including for a civil penalty of up to a specified amount per violation.

Private Sector Use; Housing

California

S 420 

Automated Decision Systems

Pending

Relates to the State AI Transparency Act and providers of generative AI systems. Relates to regulating developers or deployers of a high-risk automated decision system. Requires a developer or a deployer to perform an impact assessment on the high-risk automated decision system before making it publicly available or deploying it. Authorizes the attorney general or the Civil Rights Department to bring a specified civil action to enforce compliance.

Private Sector Use; Impact Assessment; Government Use

California

S 430 

State Government: Efficiency of Public Sector

Pending

States the intent of the legislature to enact legislation that would lessen the time, effort, money, and programmatic expertise of local public sector workers in compliance with state reporting and permit processing, as specified.

Government Use

California

S 468 

High-risk Artificial Intelligence Systems

Pending

Imposes a duty on a covered deployer, defined as a business that deploys a high-risk AI system that processes personal information, to protect personal information held by the covered deployer, subject to certain requirements.

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use

California

S 503 

Health Care Services: Artificial Intelligence

Pending

Requires developers of patient care decision support tools and health facilities, clinics, physician's offices, or offices of a group practice to make reasonable efforts to identify uses of patient care decision support tools in health programs or activities that employ input variables or factors that measure a protected characteristic. Requires, for each patient care decision support tool, developers and deployers to make reasonable efforts to mitigate the risk of discrimination.

Health Use; Notification; Audit; Oversight/Governance

California

S 524 

Law Enforcement Agencies: Artificial Intelligence

Pending

Requires each law enforcement agency to maintain a policy to require an official report prepared by a law enforcement officer or any member of a law enforcement agency to include specified information on each page, including a disclosure statement, if the report was generated in a draft, interim, or final form using AI either fully or partially.

Government Use

California

S 579 

Mental Health and Artificial Intelligence

Pending

Requires the secretary of Government Operations to appoint a mental health and AI working group that would evaluate certain issues to determine the role of AI in mental health settings. Requires the working group to take input from various stakeholder groups, including health organizations and academic institutions, and conduct at least a specified number of public meetings.

Health Use; Studies

California

S 638 

California Education and Workforce Development

Pending

Relates to the State Career Technical Education Incentive Grant Program. Provides that existing law requires an applicant to demonstrate that it, or its career technical education program, meets certain minimum eligibility standards. Revises standards related to, among other things, collaborating with labor and business entities, ensuring career programs and career pathways are aligned with workforce needs and labor market demand, and providing opportunities for certain pupil populations.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Education/Training

California

S 771 

Personal Rights: Liability: Social Media Platforms

Pending

Provides that existing law prohibits a person from using violence or intimidation to interfere with another person's enjoyment of certain rights or because of certain attributes of that person. Makes a social media platform that violates the provisions of law relating to personal rights, including through its algorithms that relay content to users, or aids, abets, acts in concert, or conspires in violation of those laws, liable for specified civil penalties.

Private Sector Use

California

S 813 

Multistakeholder Regulatory Organizations

Pending

Provides that existing law requires the Department of Technology to conduct a comprehensive inventory of all high-risk automated decision systems that have been proposed for use, development, or procurement by, or are being used, developed, or procured by, a state agency. Establishes a process by which the attorney general designates a private entity as a multistakeholder regulatory organization. Requires an MRO to perform responsibilities related to certifying the safety of AI models.

Government Use; Criminal Use

California

S 833 

Critical Infrastructure: Automated Decision Systems

Pending

Requires an operator, defined as a state agency in charge of critical infrastructure, that deploys AI to establish a human oversight mechanism to monitor the system's operations in real time and review and approve any plan or action proposed by the AI system before execution, with specified exceptions. Requires the Department of Technology to administer specialized training in AI safety protocols and risk management techniques.

Cybersecurity; Private Sector Use

California

SCR 82 

Public Higher Education: Artificial Intelligence Usage

Pending

Encourages the President of the University of California, the Chancellor of the California State University, and the Chancellor of the California Community Colleges to create a workgroup of faculty, staff, and administrators to review the use of AI in higher education, and create a report and make public the strategies and best practices for AI usage agreed upon by the workgroup.

Education Use; Education/Training

California

SCR 91 

Founding of the State of California

Pending

Commemorates the 175th anniversary of the founding of the State of California and honors the profound contributions of its people, industries, and institutions to the advancement of our state, nation, and world.

 

California

SJR 2 

Classified Workers Rights

Adopted

Urges President Donald J. Trump and the Congress of the United States to approve federal legislation guaranteeing specified rights to classified workers that would, among other things, empower those workers to work in a stable, safe environment and receive livable and competitive wages.

 

Colorado

H 1004 

Pricing Coordination Between Landlords

Vetoed

Concerns pricing coordination by landlords; prohibits landlords from using coordinators; prohibits coordinators from facilitating agreements that reduce competition between landlords; prohibits two or more landlords from engaging in consciously parallel pricing coordination.

Private Sector Use; Housing

Colorado

H 1153 

Statewide Government Language Access Assessment

Enacted

Concerns a statewide language access assessment covering principal departments; provides to identify the needs of principal departments to meet the language access standards outlined in the language access universal policy, including requests for guidance, training, and technical assistance; provides relevant language access materials from principal departments.

S 11 

Detection Components for Wildfire Mitigation 

 

Failed - Adjourned

S 22

Failed - Adjourned

Concerns the application of AI; predicts, prevents, or assists in fighting wildfires; makes an appropriation.

S 240 

Electronic Discovery in Criminal Cases Task Force

Concerns benefits to facilitate data center development while supporting electric grid infrastructure; creates the state data center development and grid modernization act.

H 5045

H 5587 

Health Insurers 

 

Failed

Prohibits any health insurer from using AI as the primary method to deny health insurance claims.

H 5590 

Artificial Intelligence 

Failed

Prohibits any health insurer from using AI to deny health insurance claims.

H 5598 

Creation and Dissemination of Artificial Intelligence

Failed

Establishes a crime prohibiting the creation and dissemination of AI generated pornographic images of a person without such person’s consent.

H 5877 

Use of Artificial Intelligence to Replace Teachers

Failed

Prohibits the use of AI to replace public school educators in providing instruction to and regular interaction with students.

H 6060 

Health Data 

 

Failed

Concerns health data; establishes additional standards governing the ownership, collection, storage and use of health data.

Connecticut

H 6478 

Use of Certain Software

Failed

Prohibits the use of certain software that uses one or more algorithms to examine data for the purpose of increasing landlord and property manager profits by constraining housing supply and increasing rental rates.

Private Sector Use; Housing

Connecticut

H 6497 

Pricing Algorithms

Failed

Prohibits residential rental property owners from using pricing algorithms and competitors' sensitive data to set rental prices.

Private Sector Use; Housing

Connecticut

H 6846 

Artificial Intelligence Deceptive Synthetic Media

Failed - Adjourned

Concerns AI, deceptive synthetic media and elections; prohibits distribution of certain deceptive synthetic media within the ninety-day period preceding an election or primary.

H 6947 

Residential Rental Property Owners From Using Pricing

Failed

Prohibits residential rental property owners from using pricing algorithms and competitors' sensitive data to set rental prices.

Connecticut

H 7209 

Legal Proceedings 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Concerns legal proceedings; involves housing matters and the impermissible use of pricing algorithms and competitors' sensitive data to set rental prices; provides additional legal protections to persons who are negatively impacted by housing discrimination, improper denials of affordable housing applications and the use of unfair business practices when calculating rental prices for residential dwelling units.

Housing; Private Sector Use; Private Sector Use

Connecticut

S 2 

Artificial Intelligence

Failed - Adjourned

Concerns AI; provides that the Technology Talent and Innovation Fund Advisory Committee shall partner with public and private institutions of higher education in the state and other training providers to develop programs in the field of AI, including, but not limited to, in areas such as prompt engineering, AI marketing for small businesses and AI for small business operations.

Responsible Use; Private Sector Use; Impact Assessment ; Cybersecurity

Connecticut

S 10 

Health Insurance and Patient Protection

Enacted

Provides that each utilization review, there shall be a rebuttable presumption that each health care service under review is medically necessary if such health care service was ordered by a health care professional acting within such health care professional's scope of practice; provides that any utilization review company performing such review of a health care service shall have the burden of proving that such health care service is not medically necessary.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Connecticut

S 348 

Unlawful Dissemination of Intimate Images

Failed

Concerns unlawful dissemination of intimate images that are digitally altered or created through the use of AI; criminalizes unauthorized dissemination of intimate images that are digitally altered or created through the use of AI.

S 447 

Health Carriers and Using Artificial Intelligence

Failed

Prohibits health carriers from using AI in the evaluation and determination of patient care to safeguard patient access to testing, medications and procedures.

Connecticut

S 817 

Software Tools and Health Insurance Claims

Failed

Prohibits any health insurer from using a software tool, including, but not limited to, AI or an algorithm, to automatically down code or deny a health insurance claim submitted by a health care provider without detailed review by a clinical peer.

Connecticut

S 1143 

Creation and Dissemination of Intimate Images 

 

Failed

Delaware

H 16 

Artificial Intelligence Commission

Enacted

Relates to the AI Commission; adds a high school student to the AI Commission as a nonvoting member; provides that the chair of the commission will appoint a student who is, or will be in the next school year, a 10th-, 11th-, or 12th-grade student in a public or private school in the state; provides that priority must be given to the selection of an individual who has demonstrated an interest in and commitment to the study of AI.

H 233 

Large Energy Use Facilities

Pending - Carryover

Requires regulated utilities to establish a separate rate for large energy-use facilities that mitigates the risk of costs associated with expanding infrastructure and maintaining reliability in the face of growing demand from being shifted to residential, small business, and other electric customers.

Provenance of Digital Content

Failed

H 1103 

Agency for Persons with Disabilities

Enacted

Failed

H 7 

Income Tax

Pending - Carryover

Relates to the imposition, rate, computation, exemptions, and credits relative to income taxes, so as to provide for a tax credit for workforce-ready graduates employed in high-tech full-time jobs in rural counties in the state; requires the Department of Labor to establish certain criteria; provides for conditions and limitations; provides for definitions; provides for rules and regulations and forms; provides for related matters.

H 147 

Technology Authority

Pending - Carryover

Provides for an annual inventory of AI usage by state agencies; Provides for an annual inventory of AI usage by state agencies; provides for annual reports of such; provides for the authority to develop and establish certain policies; designates the attorney general as an applicable elected representative who may approve the issuance of certain private activity bonds to finance or refinance state charter school facilities; provides for the establishment of procedures for public notices and hearings relative to the issuance of such bonds.

Government Use; Notification; Impact Assessment 

Georgia

H 171 

Ensuring Accountability for Illegal AI Activities Act

Pending - Carryover

Georgia

H 478 

Commerce and Trade

Pending - Carryover

Notification; Private Sector Use

Georgia

H 487 

Quality Basic Education Act

Pending - Carryover

Relates to competencies and core curriculum under the Quality Basic Education Act, so as to provide that; begins in the 2031-2032 school year, a computer science course shall be a high school graduation requirement; provides for certain computer science courses to be substituted for units of credit graduation requirements in certain other subject areas; provides for such exception; revises legislative findings; provides for related matters; repeals conflicting laws.

Education/Training

Georgia

H 566 

NO FAKES Act

Pending - Carryover

Relating to selling and other trade practices, so as to enact the NO FAKES Act of 2025; protects intellectual property rights in the voice and visual likeness of individuals; provides for the characteristics of a right to use the voice or visual likeness in a digital replica; provides for licensing and transferability of a right; requires the secretary of state to maintain a directory of certain post-mortem digital replication rights and a directory of designated agents.

Criminal Use; Private Sector Use

Georgia

H 679 

End Rental Price Fixing Act 

 

Pending - Carryover

Relates to illegal and void contracts generally, so as to prohibit certain agreements involving rental price-fixing as unenforceable contracts in general restraint of trade with respect to residential rental properties; provides for a criminal penalty; provides for statutory construction; provides for a short title; provides for an effective date and applicability; provides for related matters; repeals conflicting laws.

Housing; Private Sector Use

Georgia

H 715 

Fair and Future Ready Housing Act

Pending - Carryover

Relates to fair housing, so as to provide for AI or automated decision tools in actions for discriminatory housing practices; prohibits the use of certain defenses in actions for discriminatory housing practices; prohibits the use of AI or automated decision tools without human oversight in making certain housing determinations; prohibits making certain housing determinations using AI or automated decision tools without certain disclosures.

S 9

S 164

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use; Effect on Labor/Employment; Education Use; Health Use; Notification; Impact Assessment; Audit

Georgia

S 179 

Elementary and Secondary Education

Pending - Carryover

Relates to elementary and secondary education, so as to provide that, beginning in the 2031-2032 school year, a computer science course shall be a high school graduation requirement; provides for certain computer science courses to be substituted for units of credit graduation requirements in certain other subject areas; provides for such exception; revises legislative findings; increases the allowable charge per student per semester for each Georgia Virtual School course from $250 to $350.

Education Use; Education/Training

Georgia

S 249 

Quality Basic Education Act 

 

Pending - Carryover

Relates to competencies and core curriculum under the Quality Basic Education Act, so as to provide that, begins in the specified school year, a computer science course shall be a high school graduation requirement; provides for certain computer science courses to be substituted for units of credit graduation requirements in certain other subject areas; provides for such exception; revises legislative findings; provides for related matters; repeals conflicting laws.

Education Use; Education/Training

Georgia

S 318 

Illegal and Void Contracts Generally

Pending - Carryover

Relates to illegal and void contracts generally, so as to prohibit certain agreements involving parallel pricing coordination as unenforceable contracts in general restraint of trade with respect to residential rental properties; provides for a civil penalty; provides for educational materials informing residents in this state of the provisions of this Act; provides for statutory construction; provides for an effective date and applicability; provides for related matters; repeals conflicting laws.

Housing; Private Sector Use

Georgia

S 354 

Torts 

 

Pending - Carryover

Relates to torts, so as to provide for a cause of action for appropriating an individual’s indicia of identity; provides for a right to protection against the appropriation of an individual’s indicia of identity; provides for assignment or licensing of such right; provides for damages; provides for other remedies; provides for exceptions; provides for definitions; provides for statutory construction; provides for related matters; repeals conflicting laws.

Criminal Use; Private Sector Use

Georgia

SR 391 

AI and Digital Currency

Adopted

Creates the Senate Study Committee on AI and Digital Currency.

Studies; Appropriations

Georgia

SR 410 

Federal Railroad Administration 

 

Pending - Carryover

Urges the Federal Railroad Administration to deny CSXs request to become the first railroad to use Trip Optimizers Zero-to-Zero feature.

 

Georgia

SR 431 

Impact of Social Media and AI

Adopted

Creates the Senate Impact of Social Media and AI on Children and Platform Privacy Protection Study Committee.

Studies

Guam

B 64 

AI Regulatory Task Force

Pending

Establishes the Guam AI (AI) Regulatory Task Force to develop a comprehensive framework for the regulation of AI (AI) in the Government of Guam and private sector while protecting Constitutional and Organic Act Rights.

Studies

Hawaii

H 62 

Hospitals and Care Homes Nurse to Patient Ratios

Pending - Carryover

Implements various nurse to patient ratios at hospitals and care homes; requires the Department of Health to audit healthcare facility compliance.

Health Use; Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use

Hawaii

H 465 

Food Sales Dynamic Pricing

Pending - Carryover

Prohibits retailers from using dynamic pricing in the sale of food that is sold, or qualifies to be sold, as part of the federal Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program or Special Supplemental Foods Program for Women, Infants, and Children; establishes fines.

Responsible Use; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Hawaii

H 484 

Efficient Transitional Homes Commission

Pending - Carryover

Establishes the state Efficient Transitional Homes Commission to provide recommendations for the efficient, effective, and transparent management of transitional homes; requires annual reports to the Legislature.

Health Use; Studies; Government Use

Hawaii

H 487 

Government Efficiency

Pending - Carryover

Appropriate funds to the Legislative Reference Bureau to procure AI software and analytic services to streamline state law and regulations; requires a report to the Legislature.

Government Use; Appropriations

Hawaii

H 546 

Aloha Intelligence Institute AI

 

Pending - Carryover

Establishes the Aloha Intelligence Institute within the University of Hawaii to develop, support, and advance AI initiatives statewide.

Education Use; Education/Training; Effect on Labor/Employment

Hawaii

H 639 

Consumer Interactions with AI

Pending - Carryover

Requires corporations, organizations, or individuals engaging in commercial transactions to inform consumers when the consumers are communicating or interacting with a chatbot or other technology that mimics human interaction; requires developers that sell, offer for sale, advertise, or make available chatbots to disclose that their chatbots use AI and are capable of mimicking human behavior and textual or spoken conversation; authorizes private rights of action and statutory penalties.

Notification; Private Sector Use

Hawaii

H 665 

Data Sharing and Governance Working Group

Pending - Carryover

Establishes a data sharing and governance working group within the Office of Enterprise Technology Services; appropriates funds.

Oversight/Governance; Studies; Government Use

Hawaii

H 716 

Health Infrastructure Grant Program

Pending - Carryover

Establishes the Health Information Technology Infrastructure Grant Program within the Department of Health, to be administered by the State Health Planning and Development Agency, to support health information technology infrastructure and the implementation of interoperable health information technologies in federally qualified health centers, rural health clinics, Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program centers, and Native Hawaiian health centers.

Health Use

Hawaii

H 718 

University Faculty and Staff Positions 

 

Enacted

Appropriates funds to the University of Hawaii John A. Burns School of Medicine for full time equivalent faculty positions in the fields of cardiovascular biology, tropical medicine, and quantitative health biostatistics and an environmental health and safety officer staff position.

Hawaii

S 1136 

Insurance Ratemaking Regulations

Pending - Carryover

Establishes ratemaking regulations for insurers who base their rates on a policyholder or applicant's wildfire risk; amends the definition of prospective loss costs to incorporate catastrophe modeling instead of historical aggregate losses; prohibits insurers from basing certain insurance rates on past loss experience within or outside the state; requires insurers to provide a list of items that may be covered under a homeowners insurance policy issued or renewed.
