Idaho

S 1014 

Preventable Diseases in Infants and Newborns

Enacted

Provides that every physician or midwife shall, in making a report of a birth, state whether ocular antibiotic prophylaxis was applied to the eyes of the newborn; provides that all infants and newborns shall be tested for specified conditions as standard medical practice, including, among others, critical congenital heart disease; provides that for births occurring outside of a hospital, the birth attendant shall assure that CCHD screening is conducted through pulse oximetry within a certain period.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Oversight/Governance

Idaho

S 1067 

Artificial Intelligence 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Adds to existing law to establish limitations on regulation of AI.

Private Sector Use; Government Use

Illinois

H 31 

Freedom of Information Act 

 

Pending

Amends the Freedom of Information Act; defines automated request as a request that a public body has a reasonable belief was drafted with the assistance of AI or submitted without any specific, affirmative action taken by a human; provides that a public body shall respond to an automated request after receipt and provide certain types of notice to the requester; provides procedures for the requester to dispute having the request treated as an automated.

Government Use

Illinois

H 35 

AI Systems and Health Insurance

Pending

Creates the AI Systems Use in Health Insurance Act; provides that the Department of Insurances regulatory oversight of health insurance coverage includes oversight of the use of AI systems or predictive models to make or support adverse consumer outcomes; provides that the departments authority in an investigation or market conduct action includes review regarding the development, implementation, and use of AI systems or predictive models and the outcomes from the use of such systems.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Studies

Illinois

H 496 

Artificial Intelligence Video Interview Act

Pending

Amends the AI Video Interview Act; makes a technical change in a section concerning the short title.

Effect on Labor/Employment

Illinois

H 1252 

Insurance Code

Pending

Amends the Insurance Code; provides that the Amendatory Act may be referred to as the Motor Vehicle Insurance Fairness Act; provides that no insurer shall refuse to issue or renew a policy of automobile insurance based in whole or in part on specified prohibited underwriting or rating factors; sets forth factors that are prohibited with respect to underwriting and rating a policy of automobile insurance; sets forth provisions concerning the use of Territorial factors; provides that every insurer.

Responsible Use; Private Sector Use

Illinois

H 1427 

Algorithmics in Rent Act

Pending

Creates the Prohibition of Algorithmics in Rent Act; provides that in setting the amount of rent to be charged to a tenant for the occupancy of a residential premises, including determining any change in the amount of rent to be charged for the renewed occupancy of a residential premises, a landlord shall not employ, use, or rely upon, or cause another person to employ, use, or rely upon, an algorithmic device that uses, incorporates, or was trained with nonpublic competitor data.

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use; Housing

Illinois

H 1594 

Human Rights Act

Pending

Amends the Human Rights Act; provides that it is a civil rights violation for an employer, employment agency, or labor organization to take certain employment-related actions on the basis of an individual's weight and size; provides that it is a civil rights violation for the owner, lessee, proprietor, manager, superintendent, agent, or employee of a place of public accommodation, because of the weight and size of any person, directly or indirectly, to refuse, withhold from, or deny to any individual.

Responsible Use; Effect on Labor/Employment

Illinois

H 1806 

Wellness and Oversight for Psychological Resources Act

To Governor

Creates the Wellness and Oversight for Psychological Resources Act; adds provisions concerning the permitted use of AI under the Act; provides that a licensed professional may not allow an AI system to detect emotions or mental states; provides that all records kept by a licensed professional and all communications between an individual seeking therapy or psychotherapy services and a licensed professional shall be confidential.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Illinois

H 1859 

Public Community College Act

To Governor

Amends the Public Community College Act; provides that the board of trustees of a community college district shall require the primary instructor of a course to meet specified qualifications set forth in the Administrative Code and any other applicable rules adopted by the Community College Board; provides that a course may not, in lieu of a primary instructor, use AI or a generative AI program.

Education Use

Illinois

H 1918 

Managed Care Reform and Patient Rights Act

Pending

Amends the Managed Care Reform and Patient Rights Act; provides that any intergovernmental joint self-insurance pool providing health benefits under a specified provision of the Intergovernmental Cooperation Act shall not be included under the definition of health care plan.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Illinois

H 1937 

State Auditing Act

Pending

Amends the State Auditing Act; requires the Auditor General to conduct a survey of the Administrative Code to determine how many mandates and restrictions are imposed by the Administrative Code on persons, firms, and other entities within the private sector and units of local government and taxing bodies other than the state; allows the Auditor General to contract with a private entity to fulfill some or all of these requirements; allows the use of AI for these requirements.

Government Use; Private Sector Use

Illinois

H 2503 

School Code

Pending

Amends the School Code; requires the State Board of Education to establish the State Instructional Technology Advisory Board, which shall collaborate with the State Board of Education to provide guidance, integration, oversight, and evaluation of education technologies, including, but not limited to, AI technologies; sets forth the membership of the Advisory Board and terms; requires the State Board, with the Advisory Board, to develop standards concerning safety.

Education Use; Studies

Illinois

H 2655 

Higher Education Act

Pending

Amends the Board of Higher Education Act; requires the Board of Higher Education to prepare and submit to the General Assembly a report on the state AI education and development in public and private institutions of higher education; sets forth what information the report must contain; repeals the provisions a specified years after the effective date of the Amendatory Act.

Education Use; Studies; Education/Training

Illinois

H 3021 

Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act

Pending

Amends the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act; provides that it is an unlawful practice for any person to engage in a commercial transaction or trade practice with a consumer in which: the consumer is communicating or otherwise interacting with a chatbot, AI agent, avatar, or other computer technology that engages in a textual or Aural conversation.

Notification; Private Sector Use

Illinois

H 3028 

Computer-Generated Image or Video

Pending

Amends the Criminal Code of 2012 and the Removal of Private Compromising Images Act; defines image to include a computer-generated image or video, whether made, produced, or altered by electronic, mechanical, or other means.

 

Illinois

H 3334 

HUBZone Center of Excellence Act

Pending

Creates the HUBZone Center of Excellence Act; provides that the Department of Commerce and Economic Opportunity shall create the HUBZone Center of Excellence; provides that the HUBZone Center of Excellence shall serve as a center for HUBZone business development and provide sustainable opportunities for underserved communities.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use

Illinois

H 3503 

State Instructional Mandates Overview and Alignment Act

 

Pending

Creates the State Instructional Mandates Overview and Alignment Act; requires, beginning on the specified date, the State Board of Education to conduct a comprehensive review of all mandated units of study whenever new standards for student learning are adopted or every specified number of years, whichever happens first; removes language providing that the General Assembly shall appropriate funds necessary for the State Board of Education to carry out the requirements of the Act.

Education Use; Education/Training; Studies

Illinois

H 3506 

Large Developer

Pending

Changes references from developer to large developer; provides that a large developer that has one or more employees in the State shall not make, adopt, or enforce any rule, regulation, or policy preventing an employee from disclosing information to a government or law enforcement agency or any supervisor, principal officer, or board member related to an activity, policy, or practice of the large developer, where the employee has a good faith belief that the activity, policy.

Responsible Use; Private Sector Use; Cybersecurity; Notification; Audit; Criminal Use

Illinois

H 3529 

AI Governance Principles and Disclosure Act

Pending

Creates the High-Impact AI Governance Principles and Disclosure Act; makes findings; defines terms; requires the Department of Innovation and Technology; adopts rules regulating businesses that use AI systems; ensures compliance with the five principles of AI governance; lists the five principles of ai governance; requires the department; adopts rules to ensure that a business that uses an AI system publishes a report on the business's website, with certain requirements; provides for a civil penalty

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use; Notification

Illinois

H 3567 

Meaningful Human Review of AI Act

Pending

Creates the Meaningful Human Review of AI Act; sets forth provisions prohibiting a State agency, or any entity acting on behalf of an agency, from utilizing or applying any automated decision-making system, directly or indirectly, without continuous meaningful human review when performing any of the agency's specified functions; requires impact assessments to be performed by state agencies seeking to utilize or apply an automated decision-making system.

Government Use; Impact Assessment 

Illinois

H 3646 

Innovation and Technology Act

Pending

Amends the Department of Innovation and Technology Act; provides that the Generative AI and Natural Language Processing Task Force may hold public meetings in a hybrid format, with both virtual and in-person options to attend, as frequently as necessary to carry out its duties and shall convene and meet at the call of the co-chairpersons; provides that the Task Force shall file periodic reports with the governor and the General Assembly covering the task force's investigation into generative artificial.

Studies

Illinois

H 3720 

AI Act

Pending

Creates the Meaningful Human Review of AI Act; sets forth provisions prohibiting a state agency, or any entity acting on behalf of an agency, from utilizing or applying any automated decision-making system, directly or indirectly, without continuous meaningful human review when performing any of the agency's specified functions; requires impact assessments to be performed by state agencies seeking to utilize or apply an automated decision-making system.

Government Use; Impact Assessment 

Illinois

H 3838 

Ticket Sale and Resale Act 

 

Pending

Amends the Ticket Sale and Resale Act; provides that a ticket seller, ticket reseller, and ticket broker shall display the full price of a ticket, including all assessed fees, to a purchaser when the price of a ticket is first shown to the purchaser and shall not increase that price during the transaction with the purchaser; provides that the use of dynamic pricing in the course of selling a ticket is a violation of the provision; defines dynamic pricing.

Private Sector Use

Illinois

H 3851 

School Code

To governor

Provides that, beginning with the specified school year, cyber bullying includes the posting or distribution of an unauthorized digital replica by electronic means if the posting or distribution creates the effects of bullying; provides that bullying includes posting or distributing sexually explicit images; creates the State Bullying and Cyberbullying Prevention Fund; provides that the State Superintendent of Education may provide grants to schools to support anti bullying; defines AI.

Education Use

Illinois

H 4071 

 

Pending

Creates the Modular Homes Act; provides that no person, firm, or corporation shall establish, maintain, or operate a modular home community without a license from the Housing Authority; sets forth requirements for the location and size of modular homes; requires the authority to enforce the Act; limits home rule powers.

Housing

Illinois

HR 142 

Memorial Resolution

Adopted

Mourns the passing of Max Jordan Chiswick.

 

Illinois

S 124 

Insurance and Motor Vehicle Rates

Pending

Amends the Insurance Code; provides that the Amendatory Act may be referred to as the Motor Vehicle Insurance Fairness Act. Requires every insurer selling a policy of automobile insurance in the state shall demonstrate that its marketing, underwriting, rating, claims handling, fraud investigations and any algorithm or model used for those business practices do not disparately impact any group of customers based on race, color, national or ethnic origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, disability, gender identity, or gender expression.

Responsible Use; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 150 

Election Code and AI Advert Disclosure

Pending

Amends the Election Code; provides that, if a person, committee, or other entity creates, originally publishes, or originally distributes a qualified political advertisement, the qualified political advertisement; includes in a clear and conspicuous manner, a statement that the qualified political advertisement; generates in whole or substantially by AI; satisfies specified requirements; provides for civil penalties and exceptions to the provision.

Elections; Notification

Illinois

S 456 

AI Video Interview Act 

 

Pending

Amends the AI Video Interview Act; makes a technical change in a section concerning the short title.

Effect on Labor/Employment

Illinois

S 971 

AI Video Interview Act

Pending

Amends the AI Video Interview Act; makes a technical change in a section concerning the short title.

Effect on Labor/Employment

Illinois

S 1366 

State Government AI Act

Pending

Creates the State Government AI Act; provides that, before Jan. 1, 2028, the Department of Innovation and Technology shall adopt rules establishing policies and procedures concerning the development, procurement, deployment, use, and assessment of AI by agencies of state government; prohibits the deployment and use of AI by agencies of State government, unless permitted by rules adopted by the department; requires certain impact assessment reports; defines terms. Effective immediately.

Government Use; Impact Assessment 

Illinois

S 1425 

AI Systems and Health Insurance

Pending

Creates the AI Systems Use in Health Insurance Act; provides that the Department of Insurance's regulatory oversight of insurers includes oversight of an insurer's use of AI systems to make or support adverse determinations that affect consumers; provides that any insurer authorized to operate in the state is subject to review by the department in an investigation or market conduct action regarding the development, implementation, and use of AI systems or predictive models.

Health Use; Oversight/Governance; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 1507 

State Vehicle Code 

 

To Governor

Amends the State Vehicle Code; provides that the University of Illinois Chicago Urban Transportation Center shall conduct a study that includes, among other things, a comprehensive review of the City of Chicago’s website multiyear crash data on North and South DuSable Lake Shore Drive and the available research on potential effectiveness of cameras powered by AI in improving compliance and reducing crashes and road fatalities on North and South DuSable Lake Shore Drive.

Government Use; Studies

Illinois

S 1554 

Artificial Intelligence

Pending

Amends the Criminal Code of 2012; provides that certain forms of false impersonation may be accomplished by AI; defines AI.

Criminal Use

Illinois

S 1556 

AI and School Code 

 

Pending

Amends the School Code; requires the State Board of Education to establish the State Instructional Technology Advisory Board, which shall collaborate with the State Board of Education to provide guidance, integration, oversight, and evaluation of education technologies, including, but not limited to, AI technologies; sets forth the membership of the Advisory Board and terms.

Education Use; Studies

Illinois

S 1677 

Evaluation of Certified Employees Article

Pending

Amends the Evaluation of Certified Employees Article of the School Code; provides that a teacher evaluation plan; prohibits an evaluator from using AI tools; performs teacher evaluation tasks.

Education Use; Effect on Labor/Employment

Illinois

S 1792 

Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act

Pending

Amends the Consumer Fraud and Deceptive Business Practices Act; provides that the owner, licensee, or operator of a generative AI system shall conspicuously display a warning on the system's user interface that is reasonably calculated to consistently apprise the user that the outputs of the generative AI system may be inaccurate or inappropriate; provides that a violation of the provision constitutes an unlawful practice within the meaning of the Act.

Notification; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 1920 

School Code and Artificial Intelligence

To Governor

Provides that the State Board of Education shall develop statewide guidance for school districts and educators on the use of AI in elementary and secondary education; provides that the superintendent of education may convene stakeholders for a statewide council to consult on the further development of guidance on the use of AI in schools; provides that the State Board of Education shall encourage school districts to collect teaching resources to support American Sign Language programs.

Education Use

Illinois

S 1929 

Provenance Data Requirements Act

Pending

Creates the Provenance Data Requirements Act; provides that a generative AI tool provider shall apply provenance data, either directly or through the use of third-party technology, to wholly generated synthetic content generated by the provider's generative AI tool; sets forth additional requirements on generative AI tool providers, large online platforms, and manufacturers of capture devices; defines terms.

Provenance; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 1996 

Algorithmic Rent Fixing Prevention

Pending

Creates the Preventing Algorithmic Rent Fixing in the Rental Housing Market Act; provides that a real estate lessor, or any agent or subcontractor of a real estate lessor, shall not subscribe to, contract with, or otherwise exchange anything of value in return for the services of a real estate service provider; provides that a real estate service provider shall not facilitate an agreement to not compete between real estate lessors with respect to residential dwelling units.

Housing; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 2022 

Safe Patient Limits Act

Pending

Creates the Safe Patient Limits Act; provides the maximum number of patients that may be assigned to a registered nurse in specified situations; provides that nothing shall preclude a facility from assigning fewer patients to a registered nurse than the limits provided in the Act; provides that the maximum patient assignments may not be exceeded, regardless of the use and application of any patient acuity system.

Housing; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 2117 

Innovation and Technology Act

Pending

Amends the Department of Innovation and Technology Act; directs the Generative AI and Natural Language Processing Task Force to recommend policies and procedures concerning the procurement, deployment, and use by state agencies of systems that employ generative AI; recommends policies and procedures concerning uses by state agencies of generative AI that should be prohibited; assesses the use of generative AI by state agencies on the provision of state services. Adds a date by which the task force must deliver an addendum to its report. Adds to the Task Force the Chief Procurement Officer for General Services.

Government Use; Studies

Illinois

S 2203 

Preventing Algorithmic Discrimination Act

Pending

Creates the Preventing Algorithmic Discrimination Act; provides that, on or before Jan. 1, 2027, and annually thereafter, a deployer of an automated decision tool shall perform an impact assessment for any automated decision tool the deployer uses or designs, codes, or produces that includes specified information; provides that a deployer shall, at or before the time an automated decision tool is used to make a consequential decision, notify any natural person.

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use

Illinois

S 2255 

Surveillance Based Price and Wage Discrimination Act

Pending

Creates the Surveillance-Based Price and Wage Discrimination Act; provides that a person shall not use surveillance data as part of an automated decision system to inform the individualized price assessed to a consumer for goods or services; provides that a person shall not use surveillance data as part of an automated decision system to inform the individualized wage paid to an employee; sets forth exemptions to the provisions; provides that the attorney general shall enforce the act.

Private Sector Use; Effect on Labor/Employment; Responsible Use

Illinois

S 2259 

Medical Practice Act

Pending

Amends the Medical Practice Act; defines terms; provides that a health facility, clinic physician's office, or office of a group practice that uses generative AI to generate written or verbal patient communications pertaining to patient clinical information shall ensure that the communications meet certain criteria; provides that a communication that is generated by generative AI and read and reviewed by a human licensed or certified health care provider.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 2273 

Protect Health Data Privacy Act 

 

Pending

Creates the Protect Health Data Privacy Act; provides that a regulated entity shall disclose and maintain a health data privacy policy that clearly and conspicuously discloses specified information; sets forth provisions concerning health data privacy policies; provides that a regulated entity shall not collect, share, or store health data, except in specified circumstances; provides that it is unlawful for any person to sell or offer to sell health data concerning an individual.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 2394 

 

To Governor

Creates the First 2025 General Revisory Act; reconciles conflicts that have arisen from multiple amendments and enactments and makes technical corrections and revisions in the law.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 2398 

Wagering Act 

 

Pending

Amends the Sports Wagering Act; prohibits a sports wagering licensee from using AI to track the sports wagers of an individual; creates an offer or promotion targeting a specific individual; or create a gambling product.

Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 2419 

First 2025 General Revisory Act

Pending

Amends the Human Rights Act; adds to the definition of harassment in the Employment Article to include reproductive health decisions that is covered under the Act; provides that an employer is responsible for harassment and sexual harassment of the employer's employees by the employer's nonmanagerial and nonsupervisory employees, nonemployees, and third parties only if the employer becomes aware of the conduct and fails to take reasonable corrective measures.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Notification; Private Sector Use

Illinois

S 2510 

Appropriations

Enacted

Appropriates funds into the Workforce, Technology, and Economic Development Fund for grants, contracts, and contingent costs. Includes appropriations to the Urban Transportation Center at the University of Illinois Chicago report and study on North and South DuSable Lake Shore Drive crash data and cameras powered by AI.

Appropriations; Studies; Education Use

Illinois

S 2619 

Department of Government Efficiency Act

Pending

Creates the Department of Government Efficiency Act; provides that the Department of Government Efficiency is created to oversee, audit, and recommend improvements to all State agencies' operations, budgets, and practices; sets forth the membership and Powers and duties of the Department, including the power to conduct mandatory annual audits in all state agencies, review and recommend repealing programs or expenditures that prioritize ideology over taxpayer benefits.

Government Use; Audit

Indiana

H 1050 

Technical Corrections

Enacted

Addresses technical issues in the Indiana Code, including those related to spelling, tabulation, formatting, grammar, and cross-references; resolves technical conflicts from the 2024 legislative session; makes conforming amendments.

Studies; Judicial Use

Indiana

H 1296 

AI Inventory and Policies

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to AI inventory and policies; requires the Department of Education to establish the following guidelines and a model policy regarding school AI policies, an inventory of AI platforms that includes certain information as process and review by which teachers and school administrators may submit an AI platform for inclusion on the inventory; requires the department to, conduct a survey of teachers.

Government Use; Education Use; Education/Training

Indiana

H 1601 

Quantum Research Tax Incentive

Enacted

Relates to Quantum research tax incentives; amends the state sales and use tax exemption for data centers to include projects for investments in a quantum computing research, advanced computing, and defense infrastructure network that result in a minimum qualified investment within five years of specified amount; provides that a designating body may enter into an agreement with an eligible business to grant the eligible business a property tax exemption.

Private Sector Use; Energy; Taxes

Indiana

H 1620 

AI Disclosure

Failed - Adjourned

Requires a health care provider to disclose to a patient the provider's use of AI technology to, make or inform any decision involved in the provision of health care to the patient, or generate any part of a communication to the patient regarding the patient's health care; requires an insurer that provides accident and sickness coverage to disclose to an insured the insurer's use of AI.

Notification; Health Use; Private Sector Use

Indiana

H 1658 

Phases Out County and Township Assessor Offices

Failed - Adjourned

Eliminates elections for the office of county assessor and township assessor and phases out the offices of county assessor and township assessor as the terms of those elected to the offices expire; transfers the duties of the assessor to the county auditor at the expiration of each assessor's term; requires the department of local government finance (DLGF) to develop an automated valuation model system (AVM system) to be used by the DLGF.

Housing; Government Use

Indiana

LCR 1 

Interim Study Topics

Adopted

Relates to interim study topics.

Studies

Indiana

S 5 

AI Software Use 

 

Enacted

Provides that a state agency may use AI software to prepare certain statements; provides that an agency shall provide a report to the Budget Committee that details requests for new federal funds; provides that if a contract is required to be posted on the State transparency website, an agency shall provide the contract to the Department of Administration; provides that the Secretary of Family and Social Services and the Office of Medicaid Policy and Planning shall take certain actions.

Government Use; Studies

Indiana

S 377 

Regional Economic Development

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to regional economic development; provides certain planning and review requirements that must occur before funding may be allotted and disbursed to a state agency for purposes of regional development; specifies priorities for regional development funding commitments; repeals the statute that expires the central Indiana regional development authority.

Government Use; Private Sector Use

Indiana

S 480 

Prior Authorization Limits and Requirements

Enacted

Sets forth requirements for a utilization review entity that requires prior authorization of a health care service; provides that a claim for reimbursement for a covered service or item provided to an insured or enrollee may not be denied on the sole basis that the referring provider is an out of network provider; repeals superseded provisions regarding prior authorization; makes corresponding changes.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Government Use

D 1398

Relates to campaign materials AI disclosures.

H 451 

State Contracts

Pending - Carryover

Prohibits the state or a political subdivision of the state from entering into contracts with, or providing tax incentives or specified benefits to, certain companies that censor online content; includes effective date and applicability provisions.

H 535 

Legislative Council

Pending - Carryover

Requires the Legislative Council to enter into an agreement with a qualified person to conduct a review of Iowa's public education system; includes effective date provisions.

Iowa

H 1045 

Appropriations to Education System

Pending - Carryover

Relates to and makes appropriations to the education system; includes the funding and operation of the Department for the Blind, Department of Education, and State Board of Regents; includes contingent effective date provisions. Includes appropriations to address the state’s workforce needs in the areas of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics by expanding degree and certificate programs in the areas of AI, cybersecurity, computer science, computer engineering, data science, software engineering, and other high-demand areas related to technology, and fostering innovation in the areas of digital agriculture, manufacturing, water quality, vaccine delivery technologies, and biosciences.

HSB 294

Pending - Carryover

Relates to AI; includes the use of AI to create materials related to elections and protections in interactions with AI systems and makes penalties applicable.

Iowa

S 118 

State of Disaster Emergencies

Pending - Carryover

Relates to powers and duties applicable to state of disaster emergencies and public health disasters. Prohibits a state emergency from including authorizing the use of any drones, unmanned aerial vehicles, advanced robotics, or any artificial intelligence-based systems to enforce the proclamation

S 562 

Utilization Review Organization

Pending - Carryover

Relates to utilization review organizations, prior authorizations and exemptions, medical billing, and independent review organizations.

Includes appropriations to address the state’s workforce needs in the areas of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics by expanding degree and certificate programs in the areas of AI, cybersecurity, computer science, computer engineering, data science, software engineering, and other high-demand areas related to technology, and fostering innovation in the areas of digital agriculture, manufacturing, water quality, vaccine delivery technologies, and biosciences

Kansas

H 2183 

Concerning Crimes Punishment and Criminal Procedure

Failed - Adjourned

Concerns crimes, punishment and criminal procedure; modifies elements in the crimes of sexual exploitation of a child, unlawful transmission of a visual depiction of a child and breach of privacy; prohibits certain acts related to visual depictions in which the person depicted is indistinguishable from a real child, morphed from a real child's image or generated without any actual child involvement; provides an exception for cable services in the crime of breach of privacy; prohibits dissemination.

Criminal Use; Child Pornography

Kansas

H 2313 

Technology Produced by Certain Foreign Countries

Enacted

Relates to AI platforms; prohibits the use of AI platforms of concern on state-issued devices and networks; prohibits medical and research facilities from using genetic sequencers or operational software used for genetic analysis that is produced in or by a foreign adversary; provides that any state agency that utilizes an AI platform of concern or has an account with such platform shall deactivate and delete such account and shall no longer use the platform.

Government Use

Kansas

S 125 

Payment of Certain Claims Against the State

Enacted

Authorizes and directs payment of certain claims against the state; authorizes certain capital improvement projects, assessments and fees; authorizes certain transfers; imposes certain restrictions and limitations; directs or authorizing certain disbursements, procedures and acts incidental to the foregoing.

Appropriations; Government Use; Studies

Kansas

S 186 

Sworn Testimony in Support of Probable Cause

Enacted

Concerns crimes, punishment and criminal procedure; modifies elements in the crimes of sexual exploitation of a child, unlawful transmission of a visual depiction of a child and breach of privacy; prohibits certain acts related to visual depictions in which the person depicted is indistinguishable from a real child, morphed from a real child's image or generated without any actual child involvement; provides an exception for cable services in the crime of breach of privacy.

Criminal Use; Child Pornography

Kentucky

H 22 

Geoengineering 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Makes findings and declarations regarding the dangers of atmospheric polluting activities and the commonwealth's authority to prohibit geoengineering; defines terms; makes criminal atmospheric pollution a Class D felony; requires that a person found guilty of criminal atmospheric pollution pay a civil penalty of not less than specified amount in addition to all other penalties authorized by law; provides that each day that a person engages in criminal atmospheric pollution constitutes a separate offense.

Criminal Use; Government Use; Private Sector Use

Kentucky

H 358 

Use of Algorithmic Devices

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits the use of algorithmic devices in setting the amount of rent to be charged to a residential tenant.

Private Sector Use; Housing

Kentucky

H 498 

Use of AI by Courts

Failed - Adjourned

Requests that the Supreme Court establish a pilot project to permit participating courts to use AI for court transcription services; establishes guidelines.

Government Use; Judicial Use

Kentucky

H 672 

Protection of Information

Failed - Adjourned

Defines terms relating to AI; requires the Commonwealth Office of Technology to establish and implement policy standards for the use of AI; creates the AI Governance Committee; relates to tasks the committee with the establishment of responsible, ethical, and transparent procedures for the allowable use, development, and approval of AI for any department, program, cabinet, agency and administrative body.

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use; Cybersecurity; Private Right of Action; Criminal Use; Impact Assessment; Audit

Kentucky

S 4 

Protection of Information

Enacted

Defines terms relating to AI; provides that a department, agency, or administrative body shall disclose to the public, through a clear and conspicuous disclaimer, when generative AI, AI systems, or other AI-related capabilities are used, among other things, to render any decision regarding individual citizens or businesses within the state.

Government Use; Notification; Oversight/Governance; Studies; Elections; Private Right of Action

Kentucky

S 62 

Geoengineering 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Makes findings and declarations regarding the dangers of atmospheric polluting activities and the Commonwealth's authority to prohibit geoengineering; defines terms; makes criminal atmospheric pollution a Class D felony; requires that a person found guilty of criminal atmospheric pollution pay a civil penalty of not less than $500,000 in addition to all other penalties authorized by law; provides that each day that a person engages in criminal atmospheric pollution constitutes a separate offense.

Criminal Use; Government Use; Private Sector Use

Kentucky

SCR 142 

Commonwealth AI Consortium 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes the Commonwealth AI Consortium Task Force to design the needs, collect data, develop AI solutions, foster innovation and competitiveness, promote AI literacy, and ensure trusted AI development and governance; establishes task force membership; requires the task force to meet as needed; requires the task force to submit its findings and recommendations to the Legislative Research Commission by November 21, 2025.

Studies

Kentucky

SR 81 

Recognition Resolution

Adopted

Recognizes February 16 to 22, 2025, as Engineers Week in the Commonwealth of Kentucky.

 

Louisiana

H 1 

Appropriations

Enacted

Makes annual appropriations for Fiscal Year 2025-2026 for the ordinary expenses of the executive branch of state government, pensions, public schools, public roads, public charities, and state institutions and providing with respect to the expenditure of said appropriations. Includes appropriations to the Lafourche Parish District Attorney's Office for a partnership with Nicholls State University to enhance investigative capabilities using AI.

Maine

H 1154a 

Transparency in Consumer Transactions 

 

Enacted

Ensures transparency in consumer transactions involving AI; provides that a person may not use an AI chatbot or any other computer technology to engage in trade and commerce with a consumer in a manner that may mislead or deceive a reasonable consumer into believing that the consumer is engaging with a human being unless the consumer is notified in a clear and conspicuous manner that the consumer is not engaging with a human being.

Notification; Private Sector Use

Maine

H 1220a 

Maine Online Data Privacy Act

Pending - Carryover

Enacts the State Online Data Privacy Act.

Impact Assessment; Private Sector Use

Maine

H 1303a 

Threatened Unauthorized Dissemination of Private Images

Enacted

Provides that a person is guilty of unauthorized dissemination of certain private images if the person, with the intent to harass, torment or threaten the depicted person or another person, intentionally or knowingly disseminates, displays or publishes an image of another person in a state of nudity or engaged in a sexual act or engaged in sexual contact or an image that has been created or modified so that it appears to show the depicted person in a state of nudity or engaged in a sexual act.

Failed - Adjourned

Maryland

H 351 

State Debt

Enacted

Authorizes the creation of a state debt; provides that the proceeds are to be used for certain necessary building, construction, demolition, planning, renovation, conversion, replacement, and capital equipment purchases of the state and for grants to certain subdivisions and other organizations for certain development and improvement purposes; provides for the issuance and sale of bonds; imposes a certain tax on all assessable property in the state; relates to the State Historical Trust.

Education Use; Education/Training; Effect on Labor/Employment; Appropriations

Maryland

H 498 

Economic Competitiveness and Development Efforts

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the Department of Commerce; evaluates the potential employment and economic growth of the State's industry sectors; establishes a certain list of industry sectors and activities; considers for additional support; repeals the Maryland Economic Development Commission and Commerce Subcabinet; alters the designation, administration, and purposes of and eligibility for certain economic development programs; alters eligibility for and the calculation of certain economic development incentives.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Government Use; Private Sector Use; Appropriations

Maryland

H 504 

Excellence in Maryland Public Schools Act

Enacted

Alters the source of funds for the Blueprint for Maryland's Future Fund to include the interest earnings of the Academic Excellence Fund; directs county boards of education to provide certain funding to local workforce development boards to support the Career Counseling Program for Middle and High School Students; alters a certain reporting requirement of local workforce development boards.

Education Use

Maryland

H 525 

Fraudulent Vote Influencing

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits a person from using fraud; influences or attempts to influence a voter's voting decision; provides that fraud; includes the use of synthetic media; defines synthetic media as an image, an audio recording, or a video recording that has been intentionally created or manipulated with the use of generative AI or other digital technology; creates a realistic but false image, audio recording, or video recording.

Elections; Private Sector Use

Maryland

H 589 

AI Civil and Criminal Liabilities

 

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes that a person who intentionally, knowingly, or negligently designs or creates AI software able to cause physical injury or death is strictly liable for damages and subject to a civil penalty if the software is used to cause personal injury or death; prohibits a person from intentionally, knowingly, or negligently designing or creating AI software able to cause injury or death.

Criminal Use

Maryland

H 697 

AI in Health Insurance Decisions

Failed

Requires a health insurance carrier to submit quarterly reports to the State Insurance Commissioner on certain information related to the carrier's use of AI or automated decision-making systems; alters the information related to adverse decisions and grievances carriers are required to report to the commissioner.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Impact Assessment 

Maryland

H 738 

Major Information Technology Development Projects

Enacted

Provides that a unit of state government may not purchase, lease, contract for, or rent an information technology service or product under certain circumstances; provides that the Secretary of Information Technology is responsible for overseeing the implementation of major information technology development projects in the state; authorizes the secretary of Information Technology and the secretary of Budget and Management to withhold or reallocate major information technology development project funds.

Studies; Cybersecurity; Government Use

Maryland

H 740 

Campaign Materials Synthetic Media Usage Disclosure

Failed - Adjourned

Requires that candidates, campaign finance entities, and specified other persons, or agents of candidates, campaign finance entities, or specified other persons, that publish, distribute, or disseminate, or cause to be published, distributed, or disseminated, to another person in the state campaign materials that use or contain synthetic media include a specified disclosure in a specified manner; defines synthetic media as an image, an audio recording, or a video recording.

Elections; Notification

Maryland

H 782 

Study on Detecting Deadly Weapons in Schools

Enacted

Requires the State Center for School Safety to study how best to detect deadly weapons in public middle and high schools and how best to rapidly report the detection of weapons to a law enforcement agency; requires the State Center for School Safety to request any available assessment of the technologies evaluated under this Act.

Studies; Education Use

Maryland

H 807 

Data Governance Modernization

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes the position and responsibilities of the State Chief Data Officer; establishes the Office of Enterprise Data in the Department of Information Technology and the responsibilities of the Office; requires each unit of state government to appoint an Agency Data Officer; renames the Council on Open Data to be the Council for Open Data Advancement; alters the membership, member terms, and responsibilities of the Council.

Government Use

Maryland

H 817 

Residential Leases Rent Determination

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits a landlord from using certain algorithmic devices to determine the amount of rent to charge a residential tenant; makes a violation of the Act an unfair, abusive, or deceptive trade practice under the State Consumer Protection Act; applies the Act prospectively.

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use

Maryland

H 820 

Health Insurance Utilization Review

Enacted

Requires that certain carriers, pharmacy benefits managers, and private review agents ensure that AI, algorithm, or other software tools are used in a certain manner when used for conducting utilization review.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Maryland

H 823 

Generative AI 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Requires a developer of a generative AI system, on or before a specified date, and before the developer releases or substantially modifies a certain generative AI system, to publish on the developer's website documentation detailing the data used to train the generative AI system.

Private Sector Use; Provenance; Notification; Impact Assessment; Oversight/Governance

Maryland

H 956 

Workgroup on AI Implementation

Enacted

Establishes the Workgroup on AI Implementation; requires the Workgroup to monitor and make recommendations regarding certain issues related to AI and consumer protection.

Private Sector Use; Studies

Maryland

H 966 

AI Evidence Clinic Pilot Program

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes an AI Evidence Clinic Pilot Program within the Administrative Office of the Courts to provide expertise in AI to the circuit courts and the District Court in the form of expert testimony on the authenticity of electronic evidence that a court determines may have been created or altered using AI; authorizes the Governor, for fiscal years 2027 and 2028, to include an appropriation of a specified amount in the annual budget bill.

Government Use; Judicial Use; Studies

Maryland

H 981 

Evaluation on AI in Public Schools

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the State Department of Education to conduct an evaluation on the use and potential use of AI in public schools; requires that the evaluation consist of a survey of local school systems and a review of available systems that use AI to assist with student learning; requires the Department of Information Technology to assist the State Department of Education in performing its review.

Studies; Education Use

Maryland

H 983 

Language Assistance Program

Enacted

Establishes the Language Assistance Program; requires a local board of elections to participate in the Program if the State Board determines that there is a significant need for language assistance to voters in the county in a language other than English; requires the State Board to designate, not later than a certain date, certain languages other than English in which a local board that is required to participate in the Program must provide assistance to voters.

Education Use

Maryland

H 1089 

Building Information Guardrails Data Act of 2025

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes the Privacy Protection and Enforcement Unit within the Division of Consumer Protection in the Office of the Attorney General; establishes a data broker registry; requires certain data brokers to register each year with the Comptroller; imposes a tax on the gross income of certain data brokers for taxable years beginning after a specified date; requires the revenue from the data broker tax be used by Maryland Public Television to provide digital literacy support to students.

Criminal Use; Private Sector Use; Responsible Use

Maryland

H 1240 

AI in Health Care Decision Making

Failed

Prohibits health care providers and carriers from using AI if the AI has been designed only to reduce costs for a health care provider or carrier at the expense of reducing the quality of patient care, delaying care, or denying coverage for patient care; requires health care providers and carriers that use AI for health care decisions annually to post certain key data about the decisions on the health care provider's or carrier's website.

Health Use; Notification; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Maryland

H 1314 

Health Care Fees and AI Usage

 

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits certain insurers, nonprofit health service plans, and health maintenance organizations from using AI to automatically deny prior authorizations; prohibits health care providers from charging a fee to obtain a prior authorization from a carrier or managed care organization.

Health Use

Maryland

H 1316 

Youth-centric Technology

Vetoed

Requires the National Center for School Mental Health at the University of Maryland School of Medicine, in consultation with the State Department of Education, to develop and publish a youth-centric technology and social media resource guide for public school students in a certain manner; requires the guide to be distributed beginning in a certain school year in a certain manner and updated periodically.

Education Use

Maryland

H 1331 

Consumer Protection on AI

Failed - Adjourned

Regulates the manner in which a developer or deployer of AI must protect consumers from certain risks; requires a developer that offers to sell a certain AI system to provide certain information and make certain disclosures; requires a deployer to implement a certain risk management policy and take certain precautions to protect consumers from certain risks; requires a deployer to complete an impact assessment and make certain disclosures.

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use; Impact Assessment 

Maryland

H 1384 

Howard County Study on Detecting Deadly Weapons

Failed

Requires the Howard County Board of Education to study how best to detect deadly weapons in Howard County public middle and high schools and how best to rapidly report the detection of weapons to a law enforcement agency; requires, by a specified date, in a manner that does not jeopardize school safety, the Howard County Board of Education to report on the findings of its study to the State Superintendent of Schools and the Howard County Delegation to the General Assembly.

Education Use; Studies

Maryland

H 1391 

AI in Education 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the State Department of Education, in consultation with the State Board of Education, and the Task Force on AI in K-12 Education to develop or update certain guidance and strategies on AI for county boards of education; requires the Department, in consultation with the Department of Information Technology, to develop and update annually a list of approved AI tools.

Education Use; Studies

Maryland

H 1407 

Voice and Visual Likeness Digital Replication Rights

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes that each individual and a certain right holder have the right to authorize the use of the voice or visual likeness of the individual in a digital replica; provides that the right does not expire on the death of the individual and is transferable or licensable in a certain manner; provides that the right terminates after a certain period of time; requires an online service to designate an agent for certain purposes.

Criminal Use; Private Sector Use

Maryland

H 1425 

AI and Deepfake Representations

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits a person from utilizing certain personal identifying information or engaging in certain conduct in order to cause certain harm; prohibits a person from using certain AI or certain deepfake representations for certain purposes; provides that a person who is the victim of certain conduct may bring a civil action against a certain person.

Criminal Use; Notification; Private Sector Use

Maryland

H 1477 

Consumer Reporting Agencies 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes requirements for consumer reporting agencies that use algorithmic systems to assemble or evaluate consumer credit information on consumers for the purpose of furnishing consumer reports to third parties; requires the Commissioner of Financial Regulation of the State Department of Labor to establish certain assessment thresholds for algorithms, mandate regular training for human reviewers, and implement a certain whistleblower protection program.

Private Sector Use

Maryland

S 361 

Fraudulent Vote Influencing

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits a person from using fraud; influences or attempts to influence a voter's voting decision; provides that fraud; includes the use of synthetic media.

Elections; Criminal Use

Maryland

S 427 

Economic Competitiveness and Development Effort

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the Department of Commerce to evaluate the potential employment and economic growth of the State's industry sectors and establish a certain list of industry sectors and activities to be considered for additional support; repeals the State Economic Development Commission and Commerce Subcabinet; alters the designation, administration, and purposes of and eligibility for certain economic development programs; alters eligibility for and the calculation of certain economic development incentives.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Maryland

S 429 

Excellence in Maryland Public Schools Act

Failed - Adjourned

Alters the definitions of target per pupil foundation amount, collaborative time per pupil amount, and special education per pupil amount for certain fiscal years; alters the source of funds for the Blueprint for Maryland's Future Fund to include the interest earnings of the Academic Excellence Fund; freezes certain increases in the Concentration of Poverty School Grant Program for certain fiscal years; establishes the Collaborative Time Innovation Demonstration Grant and the Academic Excellence.

Education Use

Maryland

S 609 

Rent Determination by Use of Algorithmic Device

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits a landlord from using certain algorithmic devices to determine the amount of rent to charge a residential tenant; makes a violation of the Act an unfair, abusive, or deceptive trade practice under the Maryland Consumer Protection Act; applies the Act prospectively.

Private Sector Use; Housing

Maryland

S 655 

AI Evidence Clinic Pilot Program

Vetoed

Establishes an AI Evidence Clinic Pilot Program within the Administrative Office of the Courts; requires the Administrative Office of the Courts to develop a grant application for the Program and award grants to eligible institutions of higher education; authorizes the Governor to include in the annual budget bill an appropriation to the Program and establishing a certain limitation on the expenditure of appropriated funds.

Judicial Use; Studies; Government Use

Maryland

S 685 

Local Boards of Elections Language Assistance Program

Enacted

Establishes the Language Assistance Program; requires a local board of elections to participate in the Program if the State Board determines that there is a significant need for language assistance to voters in the county in a language other than English; requires the State Board to designate, not later than a certain date, certain languages other than English in which a local board that is required to participate in the Program must provide assistance to voters.

Elections

Maryland

S 704 

Evaluation on AI in Public Schools

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the State Department of Education to conduct an evaluation on the use and potential use of AI in public schools; requires that the evaluation consist of a survey of local school systems and a review of available systems that use AI to assist with student learning; requires the Department of Information Technology to assist the State Department of Education in performing its review; requires the Department to issue a final report on the results.

Education Use; Studies; Government Use

Maryland

S 705 

Department of Information Technology

Enacted

Provides that a unit of state government may not purchase, lease, contract for, or rent an information technology service or product under certain circumstances; provides that the Secretary of Information Technology is responsible for overseeing the implementation of major information technology development projects in the state; authorizes the Secretary of Information Technology and the Secretary of Budget and Management to withhold or reallocate major information technology development project funds.

Government Use; Studies; Oversight/Governance

Maryland

S 897 

Student Technology and Social Media Resource Guide

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the National Center for School Mental Health at the University of Maryland School of Medicine, in consultation with the State Department of Education, to develop and distribute a student technology and social media resource guide beginning in the 2027-2028 school year with additional information included in successive years; requires the Governor to include an appropriation of a specified amount for fiscal year 2027 and a specified amount for fiscal years 2028 and 2029 in the annual budget bill.

Education/Training; Education Use

Maryland

S 904 

Building Information Guardrails Data Act of 2025

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes the Privacy Protection and Enforcement Unit within the Division of Consumer Protection in the Office of the Attorney General; establishes a data broker registry; requires certain data brokers to register each year with the Comptroller; imposes a tax on the gross income of certain data brokers for taxable years beginning after a specified date.

Oversight/Governance; Private Sector Use

Maryland

S 905 

AI and Deepfake Representations

Failed - Adjourned

Prohibits a person from utilizing certain personal identifying information or engaging in certain conduct in order to cause certain harm; prohibits a person from using certain AI or certain deepfake representations for certain purposes; provides that a person who is the victim of certain conduct may bring a civil action against a certain person.

Private Right of Action; Private Sector Use; Criminal Use

Maryland

S 906 

AI in K12 Education 

 

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the State Department of Education, in consultation with the State Board of Education and the Task Force on AI in K-12 Education, to develop or update guidance on AI for county boards of education; requires the Department, in consultation with the Department of Information Technology, to develop and update annually a list of approved AI tools; requires each county board to conduct an annual inventory of systems.

Education Use; Studies; Government Use

Maryland

S 936 

High Risk AI

Failed - Adjourned

Requires a certain developer of, and a certain deployer who uses, a certain high-risk AI system to use reasonable care to protect consumers from known and reasonably foreseeable risks of certain algorithmic discrimination in a certain high-risk AI system; regulates the use of high-risk AI systems by establishing certain requirements for disclosures, impact assessments, and other consumer protection provisions.

Responsible Use; Private Sector Use; Oversight/Governance; Health Use; Housing

Maryland

S 987 

AI Health Software

Failed - Adjourned

Requires the Maryland Health Care Commission to maintain a registry of AI health software that may be distributed or operated in the State; prohibits a person from distributing or operating AI health software unless the software is registered with the Commission; prohibits a health insurance carrier from using AI to decide or directly influence a health care decision or a decision directly related to health care.

S 1025

Public Safety

Mississippi

H 384 

Higher Learning Quantum Computing

Failed

Provides that the State development authority shall establish a program to provide grants to state institutions of higher learning to assist such institutions with establishing endowments for the purpose of recruiting and employing professors of quantum computing and professors of AI engineering; provides an application process for state institutions of higher learning that desire to participate in the grant program; creates the state institutions of higher learning quantum computing.

Education Use; Education/Training; Effect on Labor/Employment; Appropriations

Mississippi

H 768 

ELVIS Act of 2025

Failed

Enacts the Ensuring Likeness, Voice and Image Security Act of 2025; stipulates that every individual has a property right in the use of that individuals name, photograph, voice or likeness for the purpose of expanding AI protections to individuals said property rights; stipulates that property rights provided in this act are exclusive to the individual; implements commercial exploitation guidelines; creates a civil action upon violation of this act.

 

Mississippi

H 1489 

Technology Innovation Fund 

 

Failed

Establishes the Technology Innovation Fund to be administered by the State Department of Information Technology Services; prescribes that the purpose of the fund is to provide financial support for technology driven projects that address critical needs, improve service delivery and foster collaboration among public and private entities; facilitates the deployment of emerging technologies such as AI, blockchain, cloud computing, and data analytics.

Government Use; Private Sector Use; Appropriations; Education/Training; Effect on Labor/Employment

Mississippi

H 1535 

AI Regulation Task Force

Failed

Establishes the AI Regulation (air) Task Force; provides for the appointment of members of the task force, including ex officio members; specifies the task force's purpose and duties as a regulatory sandbox; directs the task force to study and evaluate AI applications, risks and policy recommendations; requires that the task force will report its findings and any recommendations to the legislature annually by December 1.

Studies; Oversight/Governance; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Mississippi

S 2059 

AI in Education Task Force Act

Failed

Enacts the AI in Education Task Force Act for the purpose of evaluating potential applications of AI in k-12 and to develop policy recommendations for responsible and effective uses by students and educators; establishes the task force membership requirements and appointment criteria; provides the duties and responsibilities of the task force, including that the task force provide recommendations for incorporating AI into educational standards.

Studies; Education Use; Education/Training

Mississippi

S 2202 

Images or Audio Altered or Created By Digitization 

 

Failed

Criminalizes the unlawful dissemination or publication of an intimate or nonintimate image or audio created or altered by digitization where the image or audio is disseminated or published with intent to cause harm to the emotional, financial or physical welfare of another person and the actor knew or reasonably should have known that the person depicted did not consent to such dissemination or publication; defines terms; provides that the crimes include the use of images or audio created or altered.

Criminal Use

Mississippi

S 2220 

Right to Name Likeness and Voice Act

Failed

Creates new section, State Code of 1972, to establish a short title for the Mississippians' Right to Name, Likeness and Voice Act; creates new section, State Code of 1972, to define terms; creates a new section, State Code of 1972, to provide that every individual has a property right in their own name, likeness and voice; creates new section, State Code of 1972, to provide certain liability for persons or entities who infringe on an individual's.

Private Sector Use

Mississippi

S 2426 

AI Regulation Task Force 

 

Enacted

Establishes the AI Regulation Task Force; provides for membership, including ex officio members; directs the task force to study and evaluate AI applications, risks and policy recommendations; requires that the task force will report its findings and any recommendations to the legislature annually; authorizes funds and support for the task forces work; provides that the task force shall, among other things, review privacy and data protection laws and policies.

Studies

Mississippi

S 2437 

Prohibition of Exploitation By Deepfakes Act

Failed

Enacts the Prohibition of Exploitation By Deepfakes Act; defines terms; prohibits the use of interactive computer services to knowingly publish intimate visual depictions of identifiable individuals in certain circumstances; describes certain exceptions; prohibits use of an interactive computer service to publish a morphed image of an identifiable individual in certain circumstances; provides penalties for violations of this act; stipulates that consent for the creation of an intimate visual depiction.

Criminal Use

Mississippi

S 2443 

Technology Innovation Fund

Failed

Establishes the Technology Innovation Fund; provides the objectives of the fund; describes eligibility for applying for and receiving funding; provides for allocation of funds; requires recipients of funds to periodically make reports of certain information to the chief information officer; requires the chief information officer to submit an annual report to the governor and the legislature detailing use of the fund; stipulates that the fund shall initially be capitalized with an appropriation.

Appropriations; Education Use; Government Use; Private Sector Use

Mississippi

S 2535 

Future Innovators Act

Failed

Provides that, beginning with the entering ninth grade class, a public high school student shall, before graduation, be required to earn one unit of credit in a high school computer science course, or one unit of credit in an industry aligned career and technical education (cte) with embedded computer science course; provides the state graduation requirements that may be satisfied by either of these courses; requires that such courses include instruction on the fundamental concepts.

Education/Training; Education Use

Mississippi

S 2642 

AI Use in Political Advertisements

Failed

Amends Section 23-15-897, Mississippi Code of 1972, to require qualified political advertisements that utilize AI to disclose the use of AI to the public; defines what is considered a qualified political advertisement and AI as used in this section; clarifies what information must be present in an advertisement to satisfy the disclosure requirement.

Elections; Notification; Criminal Use

Mississippi

S 2796 

Ensuring Likeness Voice and Image Security Act

Failed

Enacts the Ensuring Likeness, Voice and Image Security Act of 2025; defines terms; stipulates that every individual has a property right in the use of that individual's name, photograph, voice or likeness; stipulates that property rights provided in this act are exclusive to the individual; implements commercial exploitation guidelines; creates a civil action upon violation of this act; stipulates that violation of this act is a class a misdemeanor.

Private Sector Use; Criminal Use

Mississippi

S 2834 

Information Technology Services Contracting

Failed

Prohibits the Department of Information Technology Services from contracting with business entities whose primary purpose is software development or AI research and development in mainland China or countries sanctioned by the United States Bureau of Industry and Security.

Government Use

Mississippi

S 3002 

Appropriation IHL General Support

Failed

Makes an appropriation to the Board of Trustees of State Institutions of Higher Learning for support of the eight universities for fiscal year 2026.

Education Use; Appropriations; Education/Training

Mississippi

S 2001 

State Institutions of Higher Learning

Enacted

Makes an appropriation to the Board of Trustees of State Institutions of Higher Learning for support of the eight universities for the specified fiscal year; relates to competitive bidding.

Education Use; Appropriations

Missouri

H 2 

State Board of Education Appropriations Bill

Enacted

Relates to State Board of Education Appropriations Bill.

Education/Training; Education Use; Cybersecurity; Appropriations

Missouri

H 5 

Appropriations Bill

Enacted

Appropriates money for the expenses, grants, refunds, and distributions of the Office of Administration, the Department of Transportation, the Department of Conservation, the Department of Public Safety, the Chief Executive's Office, and the several divisions and programs thereof to be expended only as provided in Article IV, Section 28 of the Constitution of Missouri, and to transfer money among certain funds for the period beginning July 1, 2025, and ending June 30, 2026.

Appropriations; Effect on Labor/Employment

H 116 

Media Literacy and Critical Thinking Act

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes the Media Literacy and Critical Thinking Act.

Disclosure of Intimate Digital Depictions

Failed - Adjourned

Montana

D 4290 

AI Laws

Failed

Revises AI laws.

 

Montana

H 10 

Information Technology Capital Projects

Enacted

Revises laws related to information technology capital projects; appropriates money for information technology capital projects for the biennium ending on specified date; provides for matters relating to the appropriations; provides for a transfer of funds from the general fund to the long-range information technology program account; provides for the acquisition, implementation, and configuration of new information technology systems; promotes statewide networks efficiencies.

Appropriations; Government Use

Montana

H 82 

Crimes Against Children 

 

Enacted

Creates the offense of grooming of a child for a sexual offense; revises certain crimes to include a law enforcement officer whom the offender believed to be a child; revises the offense of sexual abuse of children to include computer generated child pornography; adds the offense of grooming of a child for a sexual offense to the definition of sexual offense; provides for penalties.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

Montana

H 178 

Government Use of AI Systems 

 

Enacted

Limits the use of AI systems by state and local government; prohibits certain uses of AI systems; requires disclosure of certain uses of AI systems; requires certain decisions or recommendations to be reviewed by a human in an appropriate responsible position.

Government Use; Notification

Montana

H 513 

Property Right In Use Of Names, Voices, and Likenesses

Enacted

Revises laws related to use of name, voice, and visual likeness of individuals and penalties for unauthorized use; defines digital visual depiction as realistic depiction made for commercial use of the visual likeness of an individual that is created or altered in whole or in part using digital technology in a way that the depiction is nearly indistinguishable from an actual depiction of the individual.

Criminal Use; Private Sector Use

Montana

H 514 

Sexually Explicit Images 

 

Enacted

Criminalizes use of real and digitally fabricated sexually explicit images.

Criminal Use

Montana

H 533 

Wildfire and Real Property

Enacted

Requires insurers that use a wildfire risk score to provide the requester with certain information to enhance transparency; provides timelines for the insurer to respond; provides that an insurer that uses a wildfire risk score in connection with property evaluation in rating of residential real property shall, on the request of an insured, the insured's insurance producer, or an applicant for residential property insurance coverage for the wildfire risk score.

Private Sector Use

Montana

H 556 

Health Insurance

Failed

Revises usage of AI in certain health insurance.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Montana

HJR 4 

Interim Study of AI

Adopted

Relates to the interim study of AI.

Studies; Government Use

Montana

HJR 17 

Resolution Regarding Energy

Adopted

Relates to a joint resolution of the senate and the house of representatives of the state of Montana urging Congress and the president of the United States to undertake actions to unleash American energy by reforming and streamlining permitting obligations and repealing or revising environmental regulations and environmental reviews that do not align with national security interests.

Energy

Montana

S 25 

Use Of Deepfakes In Election Communications

Enacted

Regulates the use of deepfakes in election communications and electioneering communications; provides definitions; provides exceptions; provides for injunctive relief, actual damages, and punitive damages; provides for the filing of a complaint with the commissioner of political practices; provides penalties.

Elections; Criminal Use

Montana

S 212 

Right to Compute Act

Enacted

Provides that when critical infrastructure facilities are controlled in whole or in part by a critical AI system, the deployer shall develop a risk management policy after deploying the system that is reasonable and considers guidance and standards in the latest version of the AI risk management framework from, among others, the National Institute of Standards and Technology or another nationally or internationally recognized risk management framework for AI.

Criminal Use; Oversight/Governance; Private Sector Use

Montana

S 413 

Crime of Disclosing Explicit Synthetic Media

Enacted

Creates the crime of disclosing explicit synthetic media; provides definitions; requires certain entities to provide information to the Department of Justice on request; provides an immediate effective date and an applicability date.

Notification; Criminal Use; Child Pornography

Montana

S 452 

Online Media Manufacturers 

 

Failed

Requires disclosures of AI use by online media manufacturers.

Notification

L 77 

Ensuring Transparency in Prior Authorization Act 

 

Enacted

L 172 

Computer-Generated Child Pornography

Pending

Prohibits conduct involving computer-generated child pornography.

Nebraska

L 383 

Child Sexual Abuse Material 

 

Enacted

Provides that it shall be unlawful for a person 19 years of age or older to knowingly possess or receive any child sexual abuse material; provides that it shall be unlawful for a person under 19 years of age to knowingly and intentionally possess or receive any child sexual abuse material.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

L 615 

Political Accountability and Disclosure Act 

 

Pending

Prohibits distributing deepfakes under the Nebraska Political Accountability and Disclosure Act.

Pending

LR 49 

Congratulatory Resolution 

 

Adopted

Congratulates Jerry and Bill Adams as recipients of the distinguished NEBRASKAlander Award.

 

Nevada

A 73 

Elections 

 

Enacted

Relates to campaign practices; requires certain communications relating to an election that include synthetic media to contain a disclosure; requires a copy of certain communications relating to an election to be filed with the Secretary of State; provides penalties; provides other matters properly relating thereto.

Elections; Notification

A 126

Failed

Revises provisions relating to pornography involving minors.

A 187

A 271 

Elections

Failed

Revises provisions relating to elections.

A 290 

Medical or Dental Care

Failed

Revises provisions relating to prior authorization for medical or dental care under health insurance plans.

Nevada

A 295 

Health Insurance

Failed

Revises provisions relating to health insurance.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

Nevada

A 325 

Artificial Intelligence 

Enacted

Relates to AI; defines AI in the context of emergency management; requires emergency management plans prepared by the governor and each plan adopted by a state or local governmental agency to include provisions ensuring that final decisions regarding emergency response planning and the allocation of resources in response to an emergency are not made by AI; prohibits a public utility from using AI to make a final decision.

Government Use

Nevada

A 390 

Task Workers

Failed

Relates to labor; authorizes collective bargaining between task workers and task delivery platform providers; sets forth procedures and requirements governing such collective bargaining; requires a task delivery platform provider to provide certain information to users and task workers; requires a task delivery platform provider to ensure that a task worker is paid an amount not less than the minimum wage for each hour worked performing tasks.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Nevada

A 406 

Mental Health of Pupils

Enacted

Relates to health; requires a public school to provide certain information relating to mental health to pupils; prohibits certain uses of AI in public schools; requires that a pupil be allowed credit or promotion to the next higher grade despite absences from school in certain circumstances; deems certain absences from school to be approved absences.

Education Use; Education/Training; Health Use; Government Use; Private Sector Use

Nevada

A 531 

Education

Failed

Relates to education; revises provisions governing the transmittal of certain reports by the Commission on School Funding; requires the Department of Education to establish a dashboard to track daily attendance data for pupils; requires the superintendent of Public Instruction to appoint a task force to develop a rubric to compare certain metrics concerning pupils; requires the superintendent of Public Instruction to appoint a committee on the use of AI in education.

Education Use; Studies

Nevada

A 537 

Artificial Intelligence

Failed

Relates to the Department of Taxation; requires under certain circumstances, the department to ensure that a person is informed when the person is communicating or interacting with AI; clarifies that records and files of the Department that are generated by AI used by the department are confidential and privileged; provides other matters properly relating thereto; closes title an act relating to the Department of Taxation.

Government Use

Nevada

BDR 583 

Artificial Intelligence

Failed - Adjourned

Revises provisions relating to AI.

Failed - Adjourned

BDR 780 

AI Transparency in Health Care

Failed - Adjourned
