South Carolina

S 225 

AI Technologies

Pending - Carryover

Expresses support for the development of AI technologies in the state of state and oppose censorship or bias that would hamper the growth of these technological advancements.

 

South Carolina

S 268 

Implementation of the Service to Prevent Harm to Minors

Pending - Carryover

Provides that a covered online service shall take care in the use of a minor's personal data and in the design and implementation of the service to prevent harm to minors; provides that the online service must provide minors with easily accessible tools to limit time spent on the service and protect personal data; provides limits on how much of a minor's data the service may collect and restrict the use of such data.

Private Sector Use; Audit

South Carolina

S 304 

 

Adopted

(Senate Resolution) Recognizes and honors the University of South Carolina and the entire university of state system for its many and significant contributions to the education and culture of our citizens and to declare Tuesday, Feb. 11, 2025, Carolina Day at the state house.

Education/Training

South Carolina

S 443 

Licensed Physician 

 

Pending - Carryover

Requires a licensed physician to supervise and review healthcare coverage decisions derived from the use of an automated-decision making tool.

Health Use; Private Sector Use

South Carolina

S 554 

 

Adopted

(Senate Resolution) Congratulates Candice Herlong Pou of the state audit council upon the occasion of her retirement; commends her for 44 years of dedicated service; wishes her much continued success and fulfillment in the years to come.

 

South Dakota

S 164 

Deepfake to Influence an Election 

 

Enacted

Prohibits the use of a deepfake to influence an election and provides a penalty therefor; provides that no person, with the intent to injure a candidate, may disseminate a deepfake or enter into a contract or other agreement to disseminate a deepfake, within a specified number of days of an election, if the person knows or reasonably should know the item being disseminated is a deepfake and does not include with the deepfake the specified disclosure; provides that a violation is a Class 1 misdemeanor.

Elections; Notification; Criminal Use

Tennessee

H 433 

Criminal Offenses

Pending - Carryover

Expands the offense of unlawful exposure to include the distribution, with the intent to cause emotional distress, of an image of the intimate parts of another identifiable person or an image of an identifiable person engaged in sexually explicit conduct and the image was created or modified by means of a computer software program, AI application, or other digital editing tools.

Criminal Use

Tennessee

H 531 

Curriculum Education

Pending - Carryover

Requires local education agencies and public charter schools; provides age-appropriate instruction to students on how to access, utilize, and critically evaluate various AI tools within the curriculum taught by the educator; requires the department of education; issues guidance on how to implement the required instruction; requires the department; provides a professional development program in AI education for educators; subjects to available funding.

Education Use; Education/Training

Tennessee

H 545 

Department of Education

Pending - Carryover

Requires the department; provides at least one professional development course that provides asynchronous instruction on the use of AI in the classroom and that is made available, at no cost, to teachers in grades 6-12; requires such teachers; completes at least one department-approved professional development course on the use of AI in the classroom by the specified date, or within two years of becoming licensed as a teacher in this state, whichever is later.

Education Use; Education/Training

Tennessee

H 825 

Curriculum Education

Enacted

Enacts the Teen Social Media and Internet Safety Act, which requires the Department of Education to develop guidance for LEAs and public charter schools to instruct students in grades six through twelve who are enrolled in the LEA or public charter school on social media and internet safety; prohibit students from accessing a social media platform using the internet access provided by the LEA or public charter school, except when expressly authorized by a teacher for educational purposes only.

Education Use

Tennessee

H 849 

Statutes and Codification 

 

Pending - Carryover

Defines human being, life, and natural person for statutory construction purposes; excludes from the definition of person, life, and natural person AI, a computer algorithm, a software program, computer hardware, or any type of machine.

Personhood

Tennessee

H 933 

Department of Education

Pending - Carryover

Requires the department to establish and administer a three-year AI weapons detection system grant pilot program to award grants to eligible LEAs for the purchase of AI weapons detection systems for schools without an AI weapons detection system.

Education Use

Tennessee

H 1209 

Boards and Commissions 

 

Pending - Carryover

Provides for a new definition for the term AI for the Tennessee AI Advisory Council Act; expands the number of members on the council; directs the council to make various other changes.

Tennessee

H 1246 

Consumer Protection

Pending - Carryover

Requires the office of the attorney general and reporter to submit to the chair of the house committee having jurisdiction over consumer protection and the chair of the commerce and labor committee of the senate a report on the number of investigations into violations of the Tennessee Consumer Protection Act of 1977 in which AI was used to effectuate an unfair or deceptive act or practice affecting the conduct of any trade or commerce between the specified date.

S 404 

Criminal Offenses

Pending - Carryover

Provides that a person commits unlawful exposure who distributes an image of the intimate part or parts of another identifiable person or an image of an identifiable person engaged in sexually explicit conduct if the image was photographed or recorded under circumstances where the parties agreed or understood that the image would remain private or the image of an identifiable person was created or modified by means of a software program, AI application, or other digital editing tools.

Boards and Commissions

Pending - Carryover

Provides for a new definition for the term AI for the State AI Advisory Council Act; expands the number of members on the council; directs the council; makes various other changes.

Oversight/Governance; Studies

Tennessee

S 1363 

Consumer Protection

Pending - Carryover

Requires the office of the attorney general and reporter; submits to the Chair of the House Committee having jurisdiction over consumer protection and the Chair of the Commerce and Labor Committee of the senate a report on the number of investigations into violations of the State Consumer Protection Act of 1977 in which AI was used to effectuate an unfair or deceptive act or practice affecting the conduct of any trade or commerce between the specified date, and a specified date.

Oversight/Governance; Criminal Use

Utah

H 3 

Current Fiscal Year Supplemental Appropriations

Enacted

Supplements or reduces appropriations otherwise provided for the support and operation of state government for the specified fiscal year; provides appropriations for the use and support of higher education and certain state agencies; authorizes full time employment levels for certain internal service funds; provides appropriations for other purposes as described; provides intent language.

Appropriations; Oversight/Governance

Utah

H 121 

Health Education Amendments

Failed

Amends provisions related to health education.

Education Use; Education/Training

Utah

H 163 

Adult Probation and Parole Amendments

Enacted

Amends provisions related to the Division of Adult Probation and Parole within the Department of Corrections; establishes the Division of Adult Probation and Parole; amends provisions regarding the responsibility of the Department of Corrections to supervise individuals on probation or parole.

Judicial Use

Utah

H 168 

AI in Education

Failed

Creates the AI in Education Task Force; establishes the task force membership; establishes task force duties focused on AI in education; requires quarterly meetings and annual reporting.

Education Use; Studies

Utah

H 368 

Local Land Use Amendments

Enacted

Amends the process by which a municipality or county conducts certain plan reviews; modifies notice requirements before a public hearing on a proposed land use regulation; provides that a municipality or county may not require a private individual or entity, including a community association or homeowners association, to permanently maintain or be responsible for a public access amenity or water utility unless certain conditions apply; specifies when a bond holder may require the payment of property taxes.

Government Use

Utah

H 452 

AI Amendments

Enacted

Establishes protections for users of mental health chatbots that use AI technology; prohibits certain uses of personal information by a mental health chatbot; requires a mental health chatbot to make certain disclosures to users; provides enforcement authority to the Division of Consumer Protection; establishes requirements for creating and maintaining policies for mental health chatbots; creates rebuttable presumptions for suppliers who comply with policy requirements.

Health Use; Notification; Oversight/Governance; Private Sector Use

Utah

H 468 

Automatic License Plate Reader Amendments

Failed

Modifies and establishes requirements for the use of automatic license plate reader systems; defines terms; provides for authorized uses for automatic license plate reader systems by law enforcement agencies; and other governmental entities; establishes reporting requirements regarding the use of automatic license plate reader systems for law enforcement agencies and other governmental entities.

Government Use

Utah

H 496 

Technical Corrections to the Utah Code

Enacted

Makes technical corrections to the Utah Code; eliminates or corrects references involving repealed provisions; eliminates redundant or obsolete language; makes minor wording changes; updates cross references; corrects numbering and other errors; amends the Sunset Act and the Repeal Dates by Title Act to repeal sunset and repeal dates that have passed and taken effect; adds a coordination clause to subordinate changes in this bill that are in conflict with other legislation during the 2025 General Session.

Oversight/Governance

Utah

S 3 

Appropriations Adjustments

Enacted

Supplements or reduces appropriations otherwise provided for the support and operation of State government for the specified fiscal years; provides for budget increases and decreases for the use and support of certain State agencies, public education programs, and institutions of higher education; provides funding for the bills with fiscal impact passed in the 2025 General Session.

Appropriations; Government Use

Utah

S 180 

Law Enforcement Usage of AI

Enacted

Requires a law enforcement agency to have a policy concerning the agency’s use of generative AI; requires a police report or other law enforcement record to include a disclaimer if the report or record was created wholly or partially by using generative AI; requires the author of a police report or law enforcement record that was created wholly or partially by using generative AI to certify that the author has read and reviewed the report or record for accuracy.

Government Use

Utah

S 226 

AI Consumer Protection Amendments

Enacted

Enacts provisions related to the use of generative AI in consumer transactions and regulated services; requires certain disclosures when generative AI is used in consumer transactions and regulated services; establishes liability for violations of consumer protection laws involving AI; provides a safe harbor for certain disclosures.

Private Sector Use; Notification

Utah

S 271 

Unauthorized AI Impersonation

Enacted

Expands the scope of what constitutes abuse of personal identity to include unauthorized commercial use of simulated or artificially recreated identities; prohibits distribution of technology primarily designed for unauthorized creation of content using personal identities for commercial purposes; provides that the personal identity of an individual is abused if, among other things, the use creates a false impression that the individual participated in or approved the use; creates exemptions for fair use.

Private Sector Use; Private Right of Action

Utah

S 332 

AI Policy Act Repeal Date

Enacted

Extends the repeal date of the AI Policy Act to the specified date.

Oversight/Governance; Government Use; Private Sector Use

Utah

SJR 14 

Landlords Antitrust Lawsuit

Failed

Calls on the state Attorney General to join the antitrust lawsuit against RealPage and landlords engaged in alleged price fixing actions.

Housing; Private Sector Use

Virginia

H 30 

Budget Bill

Pending

Relates to the budget bill; provides for all appropriations of the budget submitted by the Governor of the Commonwealth; provides for a portion of revenues for the specified number of years ending respectively on the specified dates. Requires the Commissioner, Secretary of Administration and the Governor's Chief Transformation Officer is authorized to issue request for information for AI applications.

Government Use

Virginia

H 249 

Department of Criminal Justice Services

Failed

Relates to Department of Criminal Justice Services; relates to law-enforcement agencies; relates to use of generative AI and machine learning systems; provides that the Department of Criminal Justice Services shall have the power and duty to establish a comprehensive framework for the use of generative AI (AI) and machine learning systems, both defined in the bill, by law-enforcement agencies, which shall include developing policies and procedures.

Government Use; Education/Training

Virginia

H 747 

AI Developer Act

Failed

Relates to AI Developer Act established; relates to civil penalty; creates operating standards for developers and deployers, as those terms are defined in the bill, relating to AI, including avoiding certain risks, protecting against discrimination, providing disclosures, and conducting impact assessments and provides that the Office of the Attorney General shall enforce the provisions of the bill.

Private Sector Use; Impact Assessment ; Responsible Use; Health Use

Virginia

H 1051 

Public Education

Failed

Relates to public education; relates to dual enrollment and concurrent enrollment; relates to high school graduation. Provides the state Board of Education shall develop guidelines addressing how computer science and AI courses can satisfy graduation requirements.

Education/Training

Virginia

H 1525 

AI-Generated Image

Failed

Relates to unauthorized creation of image of another; relates to AI-generated image; relates to penalties; creates a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person who knowingly and intentionally creates any videographic or still image using AI of any nonconsenting person if that person is totally nude, performing sexual acts, clad in undergarments, or in a state of undress so as to expose the genitals, pubic area, buttocks, or female breast and such.

Criminal Use

Virginia

H 1642 

Criminal Justice Risk Assessments

Enacted

Relates to criminal justice risk assessments; relates to human oversight; requires that the recommendations or predictions provided by any AI-based tool, defined in the bill, utilized for criminal justice purposes, including recommendations or predictions related to pretrial release, sentencing, probation, or parole, shall be reviewed and approved by a qualified human decision maker prior to using any such recommendations or predictions in making or informing a final decision.

Government Use; Judicial Use

Virginia

H 1818 

Eligible Security Equipment

Failed

Relates to public school security equipment grant act of 2013; relates to eligible security equipment; relates to School Safety Firearm Detection Software Pilot Program established; establishes the school safety firearm detection software pilot program the Pilot Program, to be administered as a part of the public school security equipment grant act of 2013.

Education Use

Virginia

H 1870 

Residential Landlord and Tenant Act

Failed

Relates to the State Residential Landlord and Tenant Act; relates to algorithmic device services or products prohibited; relates to civil penalty; prohibits the sale of algorithmic device services or products for the purpose of setting or recommending the amount of rent to be charged to a tenant for the occupancy of a dwelling unit.

Private Sector Use; Housing

Virginia

H 2046 

High Risk AI

Failed

Relates to high risk AI; relates to development, deployment, and use by public bodies; relates to work group; relates to report; creates requirements for the development, deployment, and use of high risk AI systems, as defined in the bill, by public bodies; directs the Chief Information Officer of the Commonwealth; develops, publishes, and maintains policies and procedures; concerns the development, procurement, implementation, utilization, and ongoing management.

Government Use; Responsible Use; Oversight/Governance

Virginia

H 2047 

State Residential Landlord and Tenant Act

Failed

Relates to State Residential Landlord and Tenant Act; relates to algorithmic pricing devices; relates to study; relates to report; prohibits a landlord from, for the purpose of advising the landlord of the amount of rent to charge a prospective tenant for the occupancy of a dwelling unit, using, incorporating, or training an algorithmic pricing device; defines in the bill; restrains the rental housing market in ways that constitute an unfair method of competition.

Housing; Private Sector Use; Studies

Virginia

H 2094 

High Risk AI

Vetoed

Relates to high-risk AI; relates to development, deployment, and use; relates to civil penalties; provides that each developer of a high-risk AI system shall use a reasonable duty of care to protect consumers from any known or reasonably foreseeable risks of algorithmic discrimination arising from the intended and contracted uses.

Private Sector Use; Responsible Use

Virginia

H 2121 

Digital Content Authenticity and Transparency Act

Failed

Relates to Digital Content Authenticity and Transparency Act established; relates to civil penalty; requires a developer of an AI system or service to apply provenance data to synthetic digital content that is generated by such developer's generative AI system or service and requires a developer to make a provenance application tool and a provenance reader available to the public; requires a controller of an online service, product, or feature to retain.

Provenance; Notification; Private Sector Use

Virginia

H 2250 

Consumer Data Protection Act

Failed

Relates to consumer Data Protection Act; relates to AI Training Data Transparency Act; allows consumers to authorize a third party, acting on the consumer's behalf, to opt out of the processing of the consumer's personal data; provides that such authorization may be made using technology that indicates the consumer's intent to opt out, including a browser setting, browser extension, global device setting, or other user-selected universal opt-out mechanism.

Provenance; Private Sector Use

Virginia

H 2255 

Hospital Nursing Home Fees

Enacted

Relates to hospitals and nursing homes; relates to licensure and inspection fees; establishes the Hospital and Nursing Home Licensure and Inspection Program fund; directs the State Board of Health to require in its regulations the establishment of fees for the issuance, change, or renewal of a hospital, nursing home, or certified nursing facility license to cover the costs of operating the hospital and nursing home licensure and inspection program in a manner that ensures timely completion of inspections.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Virginia

H 2268 

Department of Law

Failed

Relates to department of Law; relates to Division of Emerging Technologies, Cybersecurity, and Data Privacy established; establishes within the Department of Law a Division of Emerging Technologies, Cybersecurity, and Data Privacy to oversee and enforce laws governing cybersecurity, data privacy, and the use of AI (AI) and other emerging technologies; requires the Division to submit an annual report to the Joint Commission on Technology and Science (JCOTS) by November 1 of each year.

Government Use

Virginia

H 2288 

Unlawful Dissemination of Intimate Images of Another

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to unlawful dissemination of intimate images of another; relates to penalties; creates a tiered system of penalties for the unlawful dissemination of intimate images of another person, an offense formerly known as the unlawful dissemination or sale of videographic or still images of another person.

Criminal Use

Virginia

H 2338 

Public Education

Failed

Relates to public education; relates to concurrent enrollment; relates to high school graduation; makes several changes relating to graduation from a public high school in the Commonwealth, including eliminating the requirement for a student to complete one virtual course in order to graduate from high school and specifying that various options and requirements relating to earning career and technical education credentials.

Education Use; Education/Training

Virginia

H 2411 

Division of Consumer Counsel

Failed

Relates to Division of Consumer Counsel; relates to duties; relates to AI fraud and abuse; expands the duties of the Division of Consumer Counsel to include establishing and administering programs to address AI fraud and abuse; provides that such programs would include establishing a statewide fraud and abuse alert system to be administered by the Division.

Government Use

Virginia

H 2433 

Law Enforcement Agencies

Failed

Relates to law-enforcement agencies; relates to use of certain technologies and interrogation practices; relates to forensic laboratory accreditation; directs the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish a comprehensive framework for the use of generative AI (AI), machine learning systems, audiovisual surveillance technologies, and custodial and noncustodial interrogations of adults and juveniles by law-enforcement agencies.

Government Use; Judicial Use

Virginia

H 2496 

 

Failed

Relates to the Department of Wildlife Resources; relates to use of a safety monitoring device; relates to Fox Hill Boat Ramp; allows the Department of Wildlife Resources to place and operate a safety monitoring device at the Fox Hill Boat Ramp in the City of Hampton for the purpose of safety monitoring on state-owned property.

Government Use

Virginia

H 2554 

AI Transparency Act Established

Failed

Establishes the AI Transparency Act; requires developers of generative AI systems made available in the Commonwealth to ensure that any generative AI system that produces audio, images, text, or video content includes on such AI-generated content a clear and conspicuous disclosure that meets certain requirements.

Private Sector Use; Notification

Virginia

H 2654 

Unlawful Dissemination of Intimate Images

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to unlawful dissemination of intimate images of another; relates to penalties.

Criminal Use

Virginia

H 2662 

Hospital Oversight Fund

Failed

Relates to health care credentialing and billing oversight; relates to Hospital Oversight Fund established; relates to Independent Credentialing Review Board established; relates to Medicaid Billing Oversight Task Force established; relates to reports; establishes a Hospital Oversight Fund, Independent Credentialing Review Board, and Medicaid Billing Oversight Task Force to fund and carry out oversight of credentialing, as that term is defined in the bill, for health care providers and Medicaid billing.

Health Use; Studies

Virginia

H 2692 

Custodial Interrogations

To Governor

Relates to custodial interrogations; relates to false statements to a child prohibited; relates to inauthentic replica documents; prohibits law-enforcement officers from intentionally making false statements or materially misrepresenting any fact, including by use of inauthentic replica documents, prior to or during a custodial interrogation of a child to secure the cooperation, confession, or conviction of such child; defines inauthentic replica documents as any documents, including computer.

Government Use; Judicial Use

Virginia

HR 506 

 

Adopted

Commends Aarav Chokshi.

 

Virginia

HR 513 

 

Adopted

Commends Andy Yang.

 

Virginia

S 30 

Budget Bill

Failed

Relates to the budget bill; provides for all appropriations of the Budget submitted by the governor of the Commonwealth; provides for a portion of revenues for the specified number of years ending respectively on the specified dates. Allows for the commissioner of the Department of Motor Vehicles to issue a request for information to gather proposals on AI technology to assist in completing day-to-day operations, including issuing drivers licenses.

Government Use

Virginia

S 385 

AI Technology In Education Study

Failed

Relates to study; relates to Board of Education; relates to work group on the use of AI technology in education; relates to report; requires the Board of Education, in collaboration with the State Council of Higher Education for Virginia, to convene a work group to study and make recommendations on guidelines for the use and integration of ai technology in education in public elementary and secondary schools and public institutions of higher education.

Education Use; Studies

Virginia

S 621 

Commission On AI

Failed

Relates to Commission on AI; relates to report; relates to sunset; creates the Commission on AI to advise the Governor on issues related to AI and make advisory recommendations based on its findings.

Effect on Labor/Employment; Responsible Use; Oversight/Governance; Studies

Virginia

S 775 

Political Campaign Advertisements

Vetoed

Relates to elections; relates to election offenses; defines synthetic media as images or audio recordings of real events relating to an identifiable individual that have been intentionally altered in a manner that would cause a reasonable person to mistakenly believe that the manipulated image or audio is a recording of a real event and to have a fundamentally different understanding or impression of the expressive content of the image or audio than that person would have.

Elections

Virginia

S 1152 

Nursing Homes and Certified Nursing Facilities

Enacted

Relates to nursing homes and certified nursing facilities; relates to professional liability insurance; specifies that the required minimum amount of professional liability coverage for nursing homes and certified nursing facilities is the amount per occurrence; requires such coverage to be noneroding, i.e., the coverage limits are not reduced by legal costs.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Virginia

S 1161 

AI Transparency Act

Failed

Relates to AI Transparency Act established; requires developers of generative AI systems made available in the Commonwealth to ensure that any generative AI system that produces audio, images, text, or video content includes on such AI-generated content a clear and conspicuous disclosure that meets certain requirements specified in the bill; requires developers of generative AI systems to implement reasonable procedures.

Notification; Private Sector Use

Virginia

S 1214 

High Risk AI

Failed

Relates to high-risk AI; relates to development, deployment, and use by public bodies; relates to work group; relates to report; creates requirements for the development, deployment, and use of high-risk AI systems by state public bodies; directs the Chief Information Officer of the Commonwealth to develop, publish, and maintain policies and procedures concerning the development, procurement, implementation, and utilization.

Government Use; Oversight/Governance; Responsible Use; Studies

Virginia

S 1260 

Threats Report Against Health Care Providers

Enacted

Relates to hospitals; relates to reports of threats or acts of violence against health care providers; requires hospitals in the Commonwealth to report any threat or battery perpetrated against a health care provider while on the premises and engaged in the performance of his duties to the Department of Health; requires hospitals to report relevant data quarterly and directs the Department to publish aggregate numbers on its website.

Health Use; Private Sector Use; Government Use

Virginia

S 1400 

Residential Landlord and Tenant Act

Failed

Relates to Virginia Residential Landlord and Tenant Act; relates to algorithmic device services or products prohibited; relates to civil penalty; prohibits the sale of algorithmic device services or products, as defined in the bill, for the purpose of setting or recommending the amount of rent to be charged to a tenant for the occupancy of a dwelling unit; prohibits a person engaged in the business of providing algorithmic device services or products for the purpose of advising a landlord of the amount.

Private Sector Use; Housing

Virginia

S 1417 

Digital Content Authenticity and Transparency Act

Failed

Relates to digital Content Authenticity and Transparency Act established; relates to civil penalty; requires a developer of an AI system or service to apply provenance data to synthetic digital content that is generated by such developer's generative AI system or service and requires a developer to make a provenance application tool and a provenance reader available to the public; requires a controller of an online service, product, or feature to retain.

Provenance; Private Sector Use; Notification

Virginia

SJR 14 

Joint Commission on Technology and Science

Failed

Relates to Joint Commission on Technology and Science; relates to study; relates to advancements in AI; relates to report; directs the Joint Commission on Technology and Science to study advancements in AI (AI), including assessing the impacts of deep fakes, data privacy implications, and misinformation; measures to ensure these technologies do not indirectly or directly lead to discrimination.

Studies; Government Use

Virginia

SJR 309 

Commendatory Resolution

Adopted

Commends Carlos Del Toro.

 

Virginia

H 6001a 

General Assembly

Enacted

Relates to the budget bill; provides for all appropriations of the Budget submitted by the Governor of the Commonwealth; provides for a portion of revenues for the specified number of years ending respectively on the specified dates. Allows for the Commissioner of the Department of Motor Vehicles to issue a request for information to gather proposals on AI technology to assist in completing day-to-day operations, including issuing drivers licenses.

Government Use

Washington

H 1197 

Fiscal Biennium Second Supplemental Appropriations

Pending - Carryover

Makes second supplemental operating appropriations for the specified fiscal biennium. Washington

S 5094 

Sexually Explicit Depictions of Minors

Pending - Carryover

Concerns sexually explicit depictions of minors.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

Washington

S 5105 

Offenses Involving Fabricated Depictions of Minors

Pending - Carryover

Relates to sexually explicit depictions of minors; provides that a person is guilty of sexual exploitation of a minor if the person, among other things, knowingly causes a minor to be photographed or part of a live performance which depicts the minor engaged in sexually explicit conduct where the minor is unconscious or unaware of the photograph or recording; provides that sexual exploitation of a minor is a class B felony.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

Washington

S 5166 

Fiscal Biennium Second Supplemental Appropriations

Pending - Carryover

Makes 2023-2025 fiscal biennium second supplemental operating appropriations. Includes an appropriation to the city of Seattle to lease space for nonprofit and academic institutions to incubate technology business startups, especially those focusing on AI and develop and teach curricula to skill up workers to use AI as a business resource.

Effect on Labor/Employment

S 5395 

Prior Authorization Determination Process

Pending - Carryover

S 5422 

Use of AI

Pending - Carryover

Allows bargaining over matters related to the use of AI.

S 5469 

Rental Housing Market

Pending - Carryover

Prohibits algorithmic rent fixing and noncompete agreements in the rental housing market.

Housing; Private Sector Use

Washington

S 5810 

Supplemental Operating Appropriations

Pending - Carryover

Makes 2025-2027 fiscal biennium operating appropriations and 2023-2025 fiscal biennium second supplemental operating appropriations. Includes appropriations to the city of Seattle to lease space for nonprofit and academic institutions to incubate technology business startups, especially those focusing on AI and develop and teach curricula to skill up workers to use AI as a business resource.

Science Scientific Research

Adopted

Supports science, scientific research, and evidence-based decision making.

West Virginia

H 2014 

Certified Microgrid Development Program

Enacted

Creates the Certified Microgrid Program administered by the Division of Economic Development; renames business expansion development program administered by the department; renames high impact business development districts certified by the department; provides that any plant or facility may participate in the business expansion development program; removes requirement that high impact business development districts be located on certain lands.

Energy; Private Sector Use; Oversight/Governance

West Virginia

H 2431 

Fourth Amendment Restoration Act

Failed - Adjourned

The purpose of this bill is to prohibit law-enforcement officers and political subdivision officials from utilizing, implementing, adopting, or continuing the use of certain specified surveillance and AI technologies.

Government Use

West Virginia

H 3137 

Economic Incentives for Data Centers

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to establishing economic incentives for data centers to locate within the state; clarifies property tax treatment for eligible data centers; creates tax exemptions; creates an application process; relates to sales and use tax; relates to business and occupation tax.

Energy; Taxes; Private Sector Use

West Virginia

H 3187 

Task Force on AI

Enacted

Relates to the West Virginia Task Force on AI; adds identification of economic opportunities to the agenda of the Task Force requiring that the Task Force submit annual electric reports.

Government Use; Studies; Education Use; Effect on Labor/Employment; Private Sector Use

West Virginia

H 3264 

Ad Valorem Property Valuation

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to the ad valorem property valuation of specialized high technology property.

HCR 94 

AI Elective Course

Failed - Adjourned

Requests a study regarding the creating of an AI elective course to be offered in high schools.

West Virginia

HCR 99

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to the Resolution to study policies aimed at preventing financial fraud and scams.

Criminal Use; Studies

West Virginia

S 198 

Crimes Against Chastity, Morality and Decency

Enacted

Creates definitions relating to intimate images; clarifies the offense of nonconsensual disclosure of intimate images includes certain fabricated intimate images; creates criminal offenses for nonconsensual disclosure of fabricated intimate images; clarifies the definitions relating to filming sexually explicit conduct of minors; creates definitions relating to filming sexually explicit conduct of minors; creates definitions relating to computer-generated child pornography.

Child Pornography; Criminal Use

West Virginia

S 484 

Synthetic Media and AI

Failed - Adjourned

Relates to disclosures and penalties associated with the use of synthetic media and AI; relates to political programming or campaign advertising.

Notification; Elections

West Virginia

S 558 

Computer Science Course Prior to Graduation

Failed - Adjourned

Requires public high school students to earn a minimum of one-half unit of credit in a computer science course prior to graduating; defining "computer science"; requires the computer science course to meet or exceed standards established by the State Board of Education; requires the course to be offered in person in a traditional classroom setting unless not feasible.

Education Use; Education/Training

West Virginia

S 583 

Economic Incentives for Data Centers

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes economic incentives for data centers to locate within state.

West Virginia

S 688 

4th Amendment Restoration Act

Failed - Adjourned

The purpose of this bill is to require law enforcement officers and political subdivision officials from irresponsibly utilizing certain surveillance technologies and AI facial recognition technologies, setting forth legislative findings, providing definitions, establishing parameters for the responsible and constitutional use of these technologies.

S 699 

Environment and Humans from Geo Engineering Experiments

Failed - Adjourned

Protects environment and humans from geo engineering experiments.

S 857 

Economic Incentives for Data Centers

Failed - Adjourned

Establishes economic incentives for data centers to locate within state.

H 1

S 1

Wyoming

SJR 8 

Political Expenditures

Failed

A joint resolution requests Congress to propose a constitutional amendment to authorize states and Congress to establish reasonable limits on political contributions made to influence elections and distinguish between natural persons and artificial entities, including corporations, unions and AI.

Related Resources

Updated December 15, 2025

NCSL 50-State Searchable Bill Tracking Databases

Find the most comprehensive and complete 50-state information in NCSL's searchable bill tracking databases.
Technology
Database
Updated March 02, 2026

Artificial Intelligence Policy Toolkit

This AI Toolkit provides a wide variety of AI resources for policymakers on the state policy landscape and considerations for different sectors and specific uses, including primers, legislative tracking, issue briefs and podcasts. AI resources in the toolkit span across many topic areas like elections, healthcare, workforce, criminal justice and more.
Technology
Updated March 01, 2026

Broadband Legislation Database

NCSL’s Broadband Database tracks legislation regarding broadband policy across state legislatures including topics of broadband funding, infrastructure, accessibility, mapping, governance, net neutrality, and other related topics. It reviews enacted and pending legislation beginning in 2023 and is updated monthly as legislation is identified by NCSL staff.
